   138→class VllmServerBackend(ABC):
   139→    @abstractmethod
   140→    def start(
   141→        self,
   142→        *,
   143→        model_name_or_path: str,
   144→        host: str,
   145→        port: int | None,
   146→        timeout_seconds: int,
   147→        extra_cli_args: list[str] | None,
   148→    ) -> VllmServerHandle:
   149→        raise NotImplementedError
   150→
   151→    @abstractmethod
   152→    def logs_tail(self, handle: VllmServerHandle, *, max_lines: int = 200) -> str:
   153→        raise NotImplementedError
   154→
   155→    @abstractmethod
   156→    def diagnostics(self, handle: VllmServerHandle, *, max_lines: int = 200) -> dict[str, str]:
   157→        raise NotImplementedError
   158→
   159→    @abstractmethod
   160→    def stop(self, handle: VllmServerHandle) -> None:
   161→        raise NotImplementedError
   162→
   163→
   164→class DockerVllmServerBackend(VllmServerBackend):
   165→    def __init__(self, docker_image: str | None, docker_run_args: list[str] | None) -> None:
   166→        self._docker_image = docker_image
   167→        self._docker_run_args = docker_run_args
   168→
   169→    def start(
   170→        self,
   171→        *,
   172→        model_name_or_path: str,
   173→        host: str,
   174→        port: int | None,
   175→        timeout_seconds: int,
   176→        extra_cli_args: list[str] | None,
   177→    ) -> VllmServerHandle:
   178→        return _start_vllm_docker_server(
   179→            model_name_or_path=model_name_or_path,
   180→            host=host,
   181→            port=port,
   182→            timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,
   183→            extra_cli_args=extra_cli_args,
   184→            docker_image=self._docker_image,
   185→            docker_run_args=self._docker_run_args,
   186→        )
   187→
   188→    def logs_tail(self, handle: VllmServerHandle, *, max_lines: int = 200) -> str:
   189→        return _docker_logs_tail(handle.docker_container_name, max_lines=max_lines)
   190→
   191→    def diagnostics(self, handle: VllmServerHandle, *, max_lines: int = 200) -> dict[str, str]:
   192→        diagnostics: dict[str, str] = {}
   193→        if handle.docker_run_cmd:
   194→            diagnostics["vLLM Docker run command (redacted)"] = handle.docker_run_cmd
   195→        diagnostics["vLLM Docker logs (tail)"] = self.logs_tail(handle, max_lines=max_lines)
   196→        diagnostics["vLLM Docker inspect"] = _docker_inspect(handle.docker_container_name)
   197→        return diagnostics
   198→
   199→    def stop(self, handle: VllmServerHandle) -> None:
   200→        container_name = handle.docker_container_name
   201→        subprocess.run(["docker", "rm", "-f", container_name], check=False, capture_output=True, text=True)
   202→
   203→
   204→class NativeVllmServerBackend(VllmServerBackend):
   205→    def start(
   206→        self,
   207→        *,
   208→        model_name_or_path: str,
   209→        host: str,
   210→        port: int | None,
   211→        timeout_seconds: int,
   212→        extra_cli_args: list[str] | None,
   213→    ) -> VllmServerHandle:
   214→        return _start_vllm_native_server(
   215→            model_name_or_path=model_name_or_path,
   216→            host=host,
   217→            port=port,
   218→            timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,
   219→            extra_cli_args=extra_cli_args,
   220→        )
   221→
   222→    def logs_tail(self, handle: VllmServerHandle, *, max_lines: int = 200) -> str:
   223→        return _native_logs_tail(handle.log_dir, max_lines=max_lines)
   224→
   225→    def diagnostics(self, handle: VllmServerHandle, *, max_lines: int = 200) -> dict[str, str]:
   226→        diagnostics: dict[str, str] = {}
   227→        if handle.log_dir:
   228→            diagnostics["vLLM native log dir"] = handle.log_dir
   229→        diagnostics["vLLM native logs (tail)"] = self.logs_tail(handle, max_lines=max_lines)
   230→        return diagnostics
   231→
   232→    def stop(self, handle: VllmServerHandle) -> None:
   233→        if handle.process is not None:
   234→            handle.process.kill()
   235→
   236→
   237→def resolve_vllm_mode(mode: Literal["native", "docker"] | None) -> Literal["native", "docker"]:
   238→    mode_str = (mode if mode is not None else os.environ.get("MARIN_VLLM_MODE", "docker")).lower()
   239→    if mode_str not in ("native", "docker"):
   240→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown MARIN_VLLM_MODE={mode_str!r}; expected 'native' or 'docker'.")
   241→    return mode_str  # type: ignore[return-value]
   242→
   243→
   244→def _resolve_vllm_backend(
   245→    mode: Literal["native", "docker"],
   246→    *,
   247→    docker_image: str | None,
   248→    docker_run_args: list[str] | None,
   249→) -> VllmServerBackend:
   250→    if mode == "docker":
   251→        return DockerVllmServerBackend(docker_image, docker_run_args)
   252→    if mode == "native":
   253→        return NativeVllmServerBackend()
   254→    raise ValueError(f"Unknown vLLM mode {mode!r}; expected 'native' or 'docker'.")
   255→
   256→
   257→def _is_object_store_path(path: str) -> bool:
   258→    parsed = urlparse(path)
   259→    return parsed.scheme in {"gs", "s3"}
   260→
   261→
   262→def _maybe_enable_streaming(model: ModelConfig) -> ModelConfig:
   263→    if model.path is None:
   264→        return model
   265→    if not _is_object_store_path(model.path):
   266→        return model
   267→    if "load_format" in model.engine_kwargs:
   268→        return model
   269→
   270→    engine_kwargs = dict(model.engine_kwargs)
   271→    # Default to the non-sharded streamer for maximum compatibility.
   272→    # `runai_streamer_sharded` only works for checkpoints that are already sharded
   273→    # into `model-rank-*-part-*.safetensors`.
   274→    engine_kwargs["load_format"] = "runai_streamer"
   275→    return dataclasses.replace(model, engine_kwargs=engine_kwargs)
   276→
   277→
   278→def _engine_kwargs_to_cli_args(engine_kwargs: dict) -> list[str]:
   279→    args: list[str] = []
   280→    load_format = engine_kwargs.get("load_format")
   281→    if load_format is not None:
   282→        args.extend(["--load-format", load_format])
   283→    max_model_len = engine_kwargs.get("max_model_len")
   284→    if max_model_len is not None:
   285→        args.extend(["--max-model-len", str(max_model_len)])
   286→    gpu_memory_utilization = engine_kwargs.get("gpu_memory_utilization")
   287→    if gpu_memory_utilization is not None:
   288→        args.extend(["--gpu-memory-utilization", str(gpu_memory_utilization)])
   289→    return args
   290→
   291→
   292→def _get_first_model_id(server_url: str) -> str:
   293→    response = requests.get(f"{server_url}/models", timeout=30)
   294→    response.raise_for_status()
   295→    payload = response.json()
   296→    data = payload.get("data", [])
   297→    if not data:
   298→        raise RuntimeError(f"No models returned from {server_url}/models: {str(payload)[:2000]}")
   299→    model_id = data[0].get("id")
   300→    if not model_id:
   301→        raise RuntimeError(f"Missing model id in {server_url}/models response: {str(payload)[:2000]}")
   302→    return str(model_id)
   303→
   304→
   305→class VllmEnvironment:
   306→    """Manage vLLM server lifecycle and lm-eval configuration."""
   307→
   308→    def __init__(
   309→        self,
   310→        model: ModelConfig,
   311→        *,
   312→        mode: Literal["native", "docker"] | None = None,
   313→        host: str = "127.0.0.1",
   314→        port: int | None = None,
   315→        timeout_seconds: int = 3600,
   316→        docker_image: str | None = None,
   317→        docker_run_args: list[str] | None = None,
   318→        extra_args: list[str] | None = None,
   319→    ) -> None:
   320→        self.model_name_or_path, self.model = resolve_model_name_or_path(model)
   321→        self.mode = resolve_vllm_mode(mode)
   322→        self.host = host
   323→        self.port = port
   324→        self.timeout_seconds = timeout_seconds
   325→        self.docker_image = docker_image
   326→        self.docker_run_args = docker_run_args
   327→        self.extra_cli_args = [*_engine_kwargs_to_cli_args(self.model.engine_kwargs), *(extra_args or [])]
   328→        self._backend = _resolve_vllm_backend(
   329→            self.mode,
   330→            docker_image=self.docker_image,
   331→            docker_run_args=self.docker_run_args,
   332→        )
   333→
   334→        self.vllm_server: VllmServerHandle | None = None
   335→        self.model_id: str | None = None
   336→
   337→    def __enter__(self) -> "VllmEnvironment":
   338→        if self.vllm_server is None:
   339→            logger.info(
   340→                "Starting vLLM environment",
   341→                extra={
   342→                    "mode": self.mode,
   343→                    "model_name_or_path": self.model_name_or_path,
   344→                    "host": self.host,
   345→                    "port": self.port,
   346→                    "docker_image": self.docker_image,
   347→                    "docker_run_args": self.docker_run_args,
   348→                },
   349→            )
   350→            try:
   351→                self.vllm_server = self._backend.start(
   352→                    model_name_or_path=self.model_name_or_path,
   353→                    host=self.host,
   354→                    port=self.port,
   355→                    timeout_seconds=self.timeout_seconds,
   356→                    extra_cli_args=self.extra_cli_args,
   357→                )
   358→                self.model_id = _get_first_model_id(self.vllm_server.server_url)
   359→                logger.info(
   360→                    "vLLM environment ready",
   361→                    extra={
   362→                        "server_url": self.vllm_server.server_url,
   363→                        "container": self.vllm_server.docker_container_name,
   364→                        "model_id": self.model_id,
   365→                    },
   366→                )
   367→            except Exception:
   368→                logger.exception("Failed to start vLLM environment", extra=self.debug_snapshot())
   369→                if self.vllm_server is not None:
   370→                    try:
   371→                        diagnostics = self._backend.diagnostics(self.vllm_server)
   372→                        for label, value in diagnostics.items():
   373→                            logger.error("%s:\n%s", label, value)
   374→                    except Exception:
   375→                        logger.exception("Failed to collect vLLM diagnostics")
   376→                raise
   377→        return self
   378→
   379→    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) -> None:
   380→        self.close()
   381→
   382→    def close(self) -> None:
   383→        if self.vllm_server is not None:
   384→            self._backend.stop(self.vllm_server)
   385→            self.vllm_server = None
   386→
   387→    @property
   388→    def server_url(self) -> str:
   389→        if self.vllm_server is None:
   390→            raise RuntimeError("vLLM server is not running in this environment.")
   391→        return self.vllm_server.server_url
   392→
   393→    def debug_snapshot(self) -> dict[str, str | int | None]:
   394→        return {
   395→            "mode": self.mode,
   396→            "model_name_or_path": self.model_name_or_path,
   397→            "host": self.host,
   398→            "port": self.port,
   399→            "server_url": self.vllm_server.server_url if self.vllm_server else None,
   400→            "docker_container_name": self.vllm_server.docker_container_name if self.vllm_server else None,
   401→            "docker_image": self.vllm_server.docker_image if self.vllm_server else self.docker_image,
   402→            "docker_run_cmd": self.vllm_server.docker_run_cmd if self.vllm_server else None,
   403→        }
   404→
   405→    def logs_tail(self, *, max_lines: int = 200) -> str:
   406→        if self.vllm_server is None:
   407→            raise RuntimeError("vLLM server is not running in this environment.")
   408→        return self._backend.logs_tail(self.vllm_server, max_lines=max_lines)
   409→
   410→    def diagnostics(self, *, max_lines: int = 200) -> dict[str, str]:
   411→        if self.vllm_server is None:
   412→            return {}
   413→        return self._backend.diagnostics(self.vllm_server, max_lines=max_lines)
   414→
   415→
   416→def _pick_free_port(host: str) -> int:
   417→    with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as sock:
   418→        sock.bind((host, 0))
   419→        return int(sock.getsockname()[1])
   420→
   421→
   422→def _detect_tpu_environment() -> bool:
   423→    """Return True when running on TPU hardware.
   424→
   425→    Ray TPU pods do not consistently set `TPU_NAME`, so we also detect the
   426→    presence of TPU device nodes and other TPU-related environment variables.
   427→    """
   428→
   429→    if os.environ.get("TPU_NAME"):
   430→        return True
   431→
   432→    # GKE TPU device plugin exposes /dev/accel* device nodes.
   433→    if glob.glob("/dev/accel*"):
   434→        return True
   435→
   436→    # Heuristic fallbacks for TPU pods / libtpu environments.
   437→    for key in (
   438→        "TPU_ACCELERATOR_TYPE",
   439→        "TPU_WORKER_ID",
   440→        "TPU_WORKER_HOSTNAMES",
   441→        "TPU_MESH_CONTROLLER_ADDRESS",
   442→        "TPU_VISIBLE_DEVICES",
   443→    ):
   444→        if os.environ.get(key):
   445→            return True
   446→
   447→    # Ray TPU workers may not expose TPU env vars/device nodes to the driver
   448→    # container, but Ray's TPUAcceleratorManager can still report topology.
   449→    try:
   450→        from ray._private.accelerators import TPUAcceleratorManager
   451→
   452→        pod_type = None
   453→        if hasattr(TPUAcceleratorManager, "_get_current_node_tpu_pod_type"):
   454→            pod_type = TPUAcceleratorManager._get_current_node_tpu_pod_type()
   455→        elif hasattr(TPUAcceleratorManager, "get_current_node_tpu_pod_type"):
   456→            pod_type = TPUAcceleratorManager.get_current_node_tpu_pod_type()
   457→        if pod_type:
   458→            return True
   459→
   460→        tpu_name = None
   461→        if hasattr(TPUAcceleratorManager, "get_current_node_tpu_name"):
   462→            tpu_name = TPUAcceleratorManager.get_current_node_tpu_name()
   463→        if tpu_name:
   464→            return True
   465→    except Exception:
   466→        pass
   467→
   468→    return False
   469→
   470→
   471→def _detect_nvidia_gpu_environment() -> bool:
   472→    for key in ("NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES", "CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES"):
   473→        value = os.environ.get(key)
   474→        if not value:
   475→            continue
   476→        if value:
   477→            return True
   478→    return bool(glob.glob("/dev/nvidia[0-9]*"))
   479→
   480→
   481→def _detect_resource_type() -> Literal["tpu", "gpu", "unknown"]:
   482→    if _detect_tpu_environment():
   483→        return "tpu"
   484→    if _detect_nvidia_gpu_environment():
   485→        return "gpu"
   486→    return "unknown"
   487→
   488→
   489→def _resolve_docker_gpu_arg() -> str | None:
   490→    raw_value = os.environ.get("NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES") or os.environ.get("CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES")
   491→    if raw_value is not None:
   492→        value = raw_value.strip()
   493→        if not value:
   494→            return None
   495→        lowered = value.lower()
   496→        if lowered == "all":
   497→            return "all"
   498→        devices = [device.strip() for device in value.split(",") if device.strip()]
   499→        if devices:
   500→            return f"device={','.join(devices)}"
   501→        return None
   502→
   503→    if _detect_nvidia_gpu_environment():
   504→        return "all"
   505→    return None
   506→
   507→
   508→def _docker_run_args_for_resource(
   509→    *,
   510→    resource_type: Literal["tpu", "gpu", "unknown"],
   511→    docker_run_args: list[str] | None,
   512→) -> list[str]:
   513→    docker_args: list[str] = []
   514→    existing_args = docker_run_args or []
   515→
   516→    has_privileged = any(arg == "--privileged" or arg.startswith("--privileged=") for arg in existing_args)
   517→    has_gpus = any(arg == "--gpus" or arg.startswith("--gpus=") for arg in existing_args)
   518→
   519→    if resource_type == "tpu" and not has_privileged:
   520→        docker_args.append("--privileged")
   521→    if resource_type == "gpu" and not has_gpus:
   522→        gpu_arg = _resolve_docker_gpu_arg()
   523→        if gpu_arg is not None:
   524→            docker_args.extend(["--gpus", gpu_arg])
   525→
   526→    if not any(arg.startswith("--shm-size") for arg in existing_args):
   527→        docker_args.append("--shm-size=100gb")
   528→    docker_args.extend(existing_args)
   529→    return docker_args
   530→
   531→
   532→def _build_docker_run_command(
   533→    *,
   534→    image: str,
   535→    model_name_or_path: str,
   536→    host: str,
   537→    port: int,
   538→    container_name: str,
   539→    env: dict[str, str],
   540→    volumes: list[tuple[str, str]],
   541→    extra_vllm_args: list[str],
   542→    docker_run_args: list[str],
   543→) -> list[str]:
   544→    """Build `docker run ... vllm serve ...` as argv.
   545→
   546→    This is split out for unit testing without invoking Docker.
   547→    """
   548→
   549→    # Avoid `--rm` so that if the container exits immediately we can still collect logs/inspect output.
   550→    # We explicitly clean up with `docker rm -f` via `VllmServerHandle.stop()`.
   551→    cmd: list[str] = ["docker", "run", "-d", "--net=host", "--name", container_name]
   552→
   553→    cmd.extend(docker_run_args)
   554→
   555→    # Volumes
   556→    for src, dst in volumes:
   557→        cmd.extend(["-v", f"{src}:{dst}"])
   558→
   559→    # Env
   560→    for key, value in sorted(env.items()):
   561→        cmd.extend(["-e", f"{key}={value}"])
   562→
   563→    cmd.append(image)
   564→    cmd.extend(
   565→        [
   566→            "vllm",
   567→            "serve",
   568→            model_name_or_path,
   569→            "--host",
   570→            host,
   571→            "--port",
   572→            str(port),
   573→            "--trust-remote-code",
   574→        ]
   575→    )
   576→    cmd.extend(extra_vllm_args)
   577→    return cmd
   578→
   579→
   580→def _require_docker_available() -> None:
   581→    if not os.path.exists("/var/run/docker.sock"):
   582→        raise RuntimeError(
   583→            "Docker socket not available at /var/run/docker.sock. "
   584→            "This job requires docker-alongside-docker (mount the socket into the Ray container)."
   585→        )
   586→    if shutil.which("docker") is None:
   587→        raise RuntimeError(
   588→            "`docker` CLI not found on PATH. Install the Docker client in the Ray image to run vLLM as a sidecar."
   589→        )
   590→
   591→
   592→def _redact_docker_run_command(cmd: list[str]) -> str:
   593→    redacted = list(cmd)
   594→    i = 0
   595→    while i < len(redacted):
   596→        if redacted[i] == "-e" and i + 1 < len(redacted):
   597→            kv = redacted[i + 1]
   598→            if "=" in kv:
   599→                key, value = kv.split("=", 1)
   600→                if key in _SENSITIVE_ENV_KEYS and value:
   601→                    redacted[i + 1] = f"{key}=<redacted>"
   602→        i += 1
   603→    return shlex.join(redacted)
   604→
   605→
   606→def _start_vllm_docker_server(
   607→    *,
   608→    model_name_or_path: str,
   609→    host: str = "127.0.0.1",
   610→    port: int | None = None,
   611→    timeout_seconds: int = 3600,
   612→    poll_interval_seconds: int = 5,
   613→    extra_cli_args: list[str] | None = None,
   614→    docker_image: str | None = None,
   615→    docker_run_args: list[str] | None = None,
   616→    container_name: str | None = None,
   617→) -> VllmServerHandle:
   618→    """Start a vLLM server in a sibling Docker container and wait until it is ready.
   619→
   620→    Raises RuntimeError/TimeoutError with helpful Docker logs on failure.
   621→    """
   622→
   623→    _require_docker_available()
   624→
   625→    resource_type = _detect_resource_type()
   626→    if docker_image is None:
   627→        if resource_type == "tpu":
   628→            docker_image = DEFAULT_VLLM_TPU_DOCKER_IMAGE
   629→        elif resource_type == "gpu":
   630→            docker_image = DEFAULT_VLLM_GPU_DOCKER_IMAGE
   631→        else:
   632→            raise RuntimeError(
   633→                f"Cannot determine default docker image for unknown resource type {resource_type!r}. "
   634→                "Please explicitly specify docker_image parameter."
   635→            )
   636→        logger.info(f"No docker_image specified; defaulting to {docker_image} for {resource_type}.")
   637→
   638→    env: dict[str, str] = _vllm_jax_env()
   639→    explain_cache_misses = os.environ.get("JAX_EXPLAIN_CACHE_MISSES")
   640→    if explain_cache_misses is not None:
   641→        env["JAX_EXPLAIN_CACHE_MISSES"] = explain_cache_misses
   642→    env["TPU_MIN_LOG_LEVEL"] = os.environ.get("TPU_MIN_LOG_LEVEL", "3")
   643→    env["TPU_STDERR_LOG_LEVEL"] = os.environ.get("TPU_STDERR_LOG_LEVEL", "3")
   644→    for key in ("HF_TOKEN", "WANDB_API_KEY"):
   645→        value = os.environ.get(key)
   646→        if value:
   647→            env[key] = value
   648→
   649→    docker_args = _docker_run_args_for_resource(
   650→        resource_type=resource_type,
   651→        docker_run_args=docker_run_args,
   652→    )
   653→
   654→    if resource_type == "tpu":
   655→        logger.info(
   656→            "Starting vLLM Docker sidecar with "
   657→            f"TPU_MIN_LOG_LEVEL={env.get('TPU_MIN_LOG_LEVEL')} "
   658→            f"TPU_STDERR_LOG_LEVEL={env.get('TPU_STDERR_LOG_LEVEL')}"
   659→        )
   660→    else:
   661→        logger.info(f"Starting vLLM Docker sidecar for {resource_type} resources.")
   662→
   663→    resolved_port = port if port is not None else _pick_free_port(host)
   664→    resolved_name = container_name or f"marin-vllm-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:10]}-{resolved_port}"
   665→
   666→    cmd = _build_docker_run_command(
   667→        image=docker_image,
   668→        model_name_or_path=model_name_or_path,
   669→        host=host,
   670→        port=resolved_port,
   671→        container_name=resolved_name,
   672→        env=env,
   673→        volumes=[("/tmp", "/tmp")],
   674→        extra_vllm_args=list(extra_cli_args or []),
   675→        docker_run_args=docker_args,
   676→    )
   677→    result = subprocess.run(cmd, check=False, capture_output=True, text=True)
   678→    if result.returncode != 0:
   679→        stderr = (result.stderr or "").strip()
   680→        stdout = (result.stdout or "").strip()
   681→        raise RuntimeError(
   682→            "Failed to start vLLM Docker sidecar.\n"
   683→            f"Image: {docker_image}\n"
   684→            f"Command: {_redact_docker_run_command(cmd)}\n"
   685→            f"Exit code: {result.returncode}\n"
   686→            f"--- stdout ---\n{stdout}\n"
   687→            f"--- stderr ---\n{stderr}"
   688→        )
   689→
   690→    server_url = f"http://{host}:{resolved_port}/v1"
   691→    handle = VllmServerHandle(
   692→        server_url=server_url,
   693→        port=resolved_port,
   694→        docker_container_name=resolved_name,
   695→        docker_image=docker_image,
   696→        docker_run_cmd=_redact_docker_run_command(cmd),
   697→    )
   698→
   699→    server_models_url = f"{handle.server_url}/models"
   700→    start_time = time.time()
   701→
   702→    try:
   703→        while True:
   704→            running = _docker_container_running(resolved_name)
   705→            if not running:
   706→                logs = _docker_logs_tail(resolved_name)
   707→                inspect = _docker_inspect(resolved_name)
   708→                raise RuntimeError(
   709→                    "vLLM Docker sidecar exited before becoming ready.\n"
   710→                    f"Container: {resolved_name}\n"
   711→                    f"Image: {docker_image}\n"
   712→                    f"Command: {_redact_docker_run_command(cmd)}\n"
   713→                    f"--- docker logs (tail) ---\n{logs}\n"
   714→                    f"--- docker inspect ---\n{inspect[:8000]}"
   715→                )
   716→
   717→            try:
   718→                response = requests.get(server_models_url, timeout=5)
   719→                if response.status_code == 200:
   720→                    return handle
   721→            except requests.ConnectionError:
   722→                # Server not ready yet.
   723→                pass
   724→            except requests.Timeout:
   725→                # Server not ready yet.
   726→                pass
   727→
   728→            elapsed_time = time.time() - start_time
   729→            if elapsed_time > timeout_seconds:
   730→                logs = _docker_logs_tail(resolved_name)
   731→                subprocess.run(["docker", "rm", "-f", resolved_name], check=False, capture_output=True, text=True)
   732→                raise TimeoutError(
   733→                    "Failed to start vLLM Docker sidecar within timeout period.\n"
   734→                    f"Container: {resolved_name}\n"
   735→                    f"Image: {docker_image}\n"
   736→                    f"Endpoint: {server_models_url}\n"
   737→                    f"Elapsed seconds: {elapsed_time:.1f}\n"
   738→                    f"--- docker logs (tail) ---\n{logs}"
   739→                )
   740→
   741→            time.sleep(poll_interval_seconds)
   742→    except Exception:
   743→        subprocess.run(["docker", "rm", "-f", resolved_name], check=False, capture_output=True, text=True)
   744→        raise
   745→
   746→
   747→def _vllm_jax_env() -> dict[str, str | Any]:
   748→    env: dict[str, str | Any] = {
   749→        "TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM": "false",
   750→        # See `_vllm_env`.
   751→        "MODEL_IMPL_TYPE": os.environ.get("MODEL_IMPL_TYPE", "vllm"),
   752→        "JAX_ENABLE_COMPILATION_CACHE": os.environ.get("JAX_ENABLE_COMPILATION_CACHE", "1"),
   753→        "JAX_COMPILATION_CACHE_DIR": os.environ.get(
   754→            "JAX_COMPILATION_CACHE_DIR",
   755→            _default_jax_compilation_cache_dir(),
   756→        ),
   757→        "VLLM_XLA_CACHE_PATH": os.environ.get(
   758→            "VLLM_XLA_CACHE_PATH",
   759→            os.environ.get("JAX_COMPILATION_CACHE_DIR", _default_jax_compilation_cache_dir()),
   760→        ),
   761→        "JAX_PERSISTENT_CACHE_MIN_ENTRY_SIZE_BYTES": os.environ.get("JAX_PERSISTENT_CACHE_MIN_ENTRY_SIZE_BYTES", "-1"),
   762→        "JAX_PERSISTENT_CACHE_MIN_COMPILE_TIME_SECS": os.environ.get("JAX_PERSISTENT_CACHE_MIN_COMPILE_TIME_SECS", "2"),
   763→    }
   764→
   765→    # Pass through vLLM knobs when callers set them in the environment.
   766→    for key in (
   767→        "VLLM_ALLOW_LONG_MAX_MODEL_LEN",
   768→        "VLLM_TPU_DISABLE_TOPK_TOPP_OPTIMIZATION",
   769→        "VLLM_TPU_SKIP_PRECOMPILE",
   770→    ):
   771→        value = os.environ.get(key)
   772→        if value is not None:
   773→            env[key] = value
   774→
   775→    return env
   776→
   777→
   778→def _default_jax_compilation_cache_dir() -> str:
   779→    return f"{marin_prefix()}/compilation-cache"
   780→
   781→
   782→def _vllm_env() -> dict[str, str]:
   783→    env = dict(os.environ)
   784→    # tpu_inference defaults MODEL_IMPL_TYPE=auto, which selects flax_nnx for many
   785→    # architectures. flax_nnx currently fails without an auto mesh context, so
   786→    # default to the vllm implementation unless the user overrides it.
   787→    env.setdefault("MODEL_IMPL_TYPE", "vllm")
   788→    # Reduce TPU runtime logging noise by default (match training defaults).
   789→    env.setdefault("TPU_MIN_LOG_LEVEL", "3")
   790→    env.setdefault("TPU_STDERR_LOG_LEVEL", "3")
   791→    env.setdefault("JAX_ENABLE_COMPILATION_CACHE", "1")
   792→    env.setdefault("JAX_COMPILATION_CACHE_DIR", _default_jax_compilation_cache_dir())
   793→    # vllm-tpu uses XLA compilation caches; this env var is the one it keys off.
   794→    env.setdefault("VLLM_XLA_CACHE_PATH", env["JAX_COMPILATION_CACHE_DIR"])
   795→    # Cache aggressively for iterative bring-up workflows.
   796→    env.setdefault("JAX_PERSISTENT_CACHE_MIN_ENTRY_SIZE_BYTES", "-1")
   797→    env.setdefault("JAX_PERSISTENT_CACHE_MIN_COMPILE_TIME_SECS", "2")
   798→    return env
   799→
   800→
   801→def _start_vllm_native_server(
   802→    *,
   803→    model_name_or_path: str,
   804→    host: str = "127.0.0.1",
   805→    port: int | None = None,
   806→    timeout_seconds: int = 3600,
   807→    extra_cli_args: list[str] | None = None,
   808→) -> VllmServerHandle:
   809→    """Start `vllm serve` in-process and wait until `/v1/models` responds."""
   810→
   811→    resolved_port = port if port is not None else 8000
   812→
   813→    vllm_bin = shutil.which("vllm") or "vllm"
   814→    cmd: list[str] = [
   815→        vllm_bin,
   816→        "serve",
   817→        model_name_or_path,
   818→        "--trust-remote-code",
   819→        "--host",
   820→        host,
   821→        "--port",
   822→        str(resolved_port),
   823→        *(extra_cli_args or []),
   824→    ]
   825→
   826→    log_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="vllm_server_")
   827→    stdout_path = os.path.join(log_dir, "stdout.log")
   828→    stderr_path = os.path.join(log_dir, "stderr.log")
   829→    stdout_f = open(stdout_path, "w")
   830→    stderr_f = open(stderr_path, "w")
   831→    native_env = _vllm_env()
   832→    logger.info(
   833→        "Starting vLLM native server with "
   834→        f"TPU_MIN_LOG_LEVEL={native_env.get('TPU_MIN_LOG_LEVEL')} "
   835→        f"TPU_STDERR_LOG_LEVEL={native_env.get('TPU_STDERR_LOG_LEVEL')}"
   836→    )
   837→    process = subprocess.Popen(cmd, stdout=stdout_f, stderr=stderr_f, text=True, env=native_env)
   838→
   839→    server_url: str = f"http://{host}:{resolved_port}/v1"
   840→    start_time: float = time.time()
   841→
   842→    while True:
   843→        if process.poll() is not None:
   844→            stdout_f.close()
   845→            stderr_f.close()
   846→            logs = _native_logs_tail(log_dir)
   847→            raise RuntimeError(
   848→                "vLLM server process exited before becoming ready.\n"
   849→                f"Command: {cmd}\n"
   850→                f"Exit code: {process.returncode}\n"
   851→                f"Logs: {log_dir}\n"
   852→                f"{logs}"
   853→            )
   854→
   855→        try:
   856→            response = requests.get(f"{server_url}/models", timeout=5)
   857→            if response.status_code == 200:
   858→                break
   859→        except requests.ConnectionError:
   860→            # Server not ready yet.
   861→            pass
   862→        except requests.Timeout:
   863→            # Server not ready yet.
   864→            pass
   865→
   866→        elapsed_time = time.time() - start_time
   867→        if elapsed_time > timeout_seconds:
   868→            process.kill()
   869→            stdout_f.close()
   870→            stderr_f.close()
   871→            logs = _native_logs_tail(log_dir)
   872→            raise TimeoutError("Failed to start vLLM server within timeout period.\n" f"Logs: {log_dir}\n" f"{logs}")
   873→
   874→        time.sleep(5)
   875→
   876→    stdout_f.close()
   877→    stderr_f.close()
   878→    return VllmServerHandle(
   879→        server_url=server_url,
   880→        port=resolved_port,
   881→        process=process,
   882→        log_dir=log_dir,

   162→# TPU configurations are complicated. The number of chips per host is
   163→# always the same for a particular generation, but the number of VMs per host
   164→# can vary based on the pod size.
   165→#
   166→# Even more confusingly, Google sometimes refers to TPU cores
   167→# as chips and vice-versa: v4 and v5p topologies refer to "core", but
   168→# v5e and v6e topologies refer to "chips". It's doubly confusing as some
   169→# topologies split 2 VMs per host, while others do not. We just write them
   170→# all down here.
   171→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   172→class TpuTopologyInfo:
   173→    name: str
   174→    chip_count: int
   175→    host_count: int
   176→    vm_count: int
   177→    chips_per_vm: int
   178→
   179→
   180→TPU_TOPOLOGIES: list[TpuTopologyInfo] = [
   181→    # https://cloud.google.com/tpu/docs/v4
   182→    TpuTopologyInfo("v4-8", 4, 1, 1, 4),
   183→    TpuTopologyInfo("v4-16", 8, 2, 2, 4),

   191→    TpuTopologyInfo("v4-4096", 2048, 512, 512, 4),

   227→def get_tpu_topology(tpu_type: str) -> TpuTopologyInfo:
   228→    """Get TPU topology by type name."""
   229→    for config in TPU_TOPOLOGIES:
   230→        if config.name == tpu_type:
   231→            return config
   232→    raise ValueError(f"Unknown TPU type: {tpu_type}")

   238→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   239→class CpuConfig:
   240→    """CPU-only device configuration."""
   241→
   242→    kind: DeviceKind = "cpu"
   243→    variant: str = "cpu"
   244→
   245→    def chip_count(self) -> int:
   246→        """CPU has no accelerator chips."""
   247→        return 0
   248→
   249→    def device_flops(self, dtype: str = "bf16") -> float:
   250→        """CPU FLOPS not tracked."""
   251→        raise NotImplementedError("CPU FLOPS not available")

   253→    def default_env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:
   254→        return {"JAX_PLATFORMS": "cpu"}

   256→
   257→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   258→class GpuConfig:
   259→    """GPU device configuration."""
   260→
   261→    variant: GpuType
   262→    kind: DeviceKind = "gpu"
   263→    count: int = 1
   264→
   265→    def chip_count(self) -> int:
   266→        """Total number of GPU chips."""
   267→        return self.count
   268→
   269→    def device_flops(self, dtype: str = "bf16") -> float:
   270→        """Peak FLOP/s for a single GPU."""
   271→        from fray.v1.cluster.device_flops import device_flops
   272→
   273→        flops = device_flops(self.variant, dtype)
   274→        if flops is None:
   275→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown device/dtype: {self.variant}/{dtype}")
   276→        return flops
   277→
   278→    def total_flops(self, dtype: str = "bf16") -> float:
   279→        """Total peak FLOP/s across all GPUs."""
   280→        return self.device_flops(dtype) * self.count
   281→
   282→    def default_env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:
   283→        return {"JAX_PLATFORMS": ""}
   284→
   285→
   286→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   287→class TpuConfig:
   288→    """TPU device configuration.
   289→
   290→    Args:
   291→        variant: TPU accelerator type (e.g., "v5litepod-16", "v4-8")
   292→        topology: Optional topology specification (e.g., "2x2x1")
   293→    """
   294→
   295→    variant: TpuType
   296→    kind: DeviceKind = "tpu"
   297→    topology: str | None = None
   298→
   299→    def chip_count(self) -> int:
   300→        """Total number of TPU chips."""
   301→        return get_tpu_topology(self.variant).chip_count
   302→
   303→    def vm_count(self) -> int:
   304→        """Number of VMs in the TPU pod."""
   305→        return get_tpu_topology(self.variant).vm_count
   306→
   307→    def device_flops(self, dtype: str = "bf16") -> float:
   308→        """Peak FLOP/s for a single TPU chip."""
   309→        from fray.v1.cluster.device_flops import device_flops
   310→
   311→        flops = device_flops(self.variant, dtype)
   312→        if flops is None:
   313→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown device/dtype: {self.variant}/{dtype}")
   314→        return flops
   315→
   316→    def total_flops(self, dtype: str = "bf16") -> float:
   317→        """Total peak FLOP/s across all TPU chips."""
   318→        return self.device_flops(dtype) * self.chip_count()
   319→
   320→    def default_env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:
   321→        defaults: dict[str, str] = {"JAX_PLATFORMS": ""}
   322→        if self.variant.startswith(("v5litepod-", "v5e-", "v5p-")):
   323→            defaults["LIBTPU_INIT_ARGS"] = "--xla_tpu_scoped_vmem_limit_kib=50000"
   324→        elif self.variant.startswith("v6e-"):
   325→            defaults["LIBTPU_INIT_ARGS"] = "--xla_tpu_scoped_vmem_limit_kib=98304"
   326→        return defaults
   327→

   332→@dataclass
   333→class ResourceConfig:
   334→    """Resource requirements for a job.
   335→
   336→    Args:
   337→        cpu: Number of CPU cores
   338→        ram: RAM requirement (e.g., "8g", "16g")
   339→        disk: Disk space requirement (e.g., "10g", "100g")
   340→        device: Device configuration (CPU, GPU, or TPU)
   341→        replicas: Number of replicas/slices (for multislice TPU training)
   342→        preemptible: Whether the job can be preempted
   343→        regions: Preferred cloud regions for job placement
   344→    """
   345→
   346→    cpu: int = 1
   347→    ram: str = "128m"
   348→    disk: str = "1g"
   349→    device: DeviceConfig = field(default_factory=CpuConfig)
   350→    replicas: int = 1
   351→    preemptible: bool = True
   352→    regions: Sequence[str] | None = None
   353→
   354→    def chip_count(self) -> int:
   355→        """Total accelerator chips across all replicas/slices."""
   356→        return self.device.chip_count() * self.replicas
   357→
   358→    def device_flops(self, dtype: str = "bf16") -> float:
   359→        """Peak FLOP/s for a single device."""
   360→        return self.device.device_flops(dtype)
   361→
   362→    def total_flops(self, dtype: str = "bf16") -> float:
   363→        """Total peak FLOP/s across all devices."""
   364→        if isinstance(self.device, CpuConfig):
   365→            # just use some reasonable number
   366→            return 100e9
   367→        return self.device_flops(dtype) * self.chip_count()
   368→
   369→    @staticmethod
   370→    def with_tpu(tpu_type: str, slice_count: int = 1, **kwargs) -> ResourceConfig:
   371→        device = TpuConfig(variant=tpu_type)
   372→        return ResourceConfig(device=device, replicas=slice_count, **kwargs)
   373→
   374→    @staticmethod
   375→    def with_gpu(gpu_type: str, count: int = 1, **kwargs) -> ResourceConfig:
   376→        device = GpuConfig(variant=gpu_type, count=count)
   377→        return ResourceConfig(device=device, **kwargs)
   378→
   379→    @staticmethod
   380→    def with_cpu(**kwargs) -> ResourceConfig:
   381→        return ResourceConfig(device=CpuConfig(), **kwargs)

   385→class EnvironmentConfig:
   386→    """Job environment configuration.
   387→
   388→    Can specify either a workspace (for uv-based dependency resolution)
   389→    or a docker image (for containerized execution).
   390→
   391→    Args:
   392→        workspace: Path to workspace root for uv-based execution
   393→        docker_image: Docker image for containerized execution
   394→        pip_packages: Additional pip packages to install
   395→        env_vars: Environment variables to set
   396→        extras: Extra dependency groups for uv (e.g., ["tpu", "eval"])

   398→
   399→    workspace: str | None = None
   400→    docker_image: str | None = None
   401→    pip_packages: Sequence[str] = field(default_factory=list)
   402→    env_vars: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
   403→    extras: Sequence[str] = field(default_factory=list)
   404→
   405→    def __post_init__(self):
   406→        if self.workspace and self.docker_image:

   408→        if not self.workspace and not self.docker_image:
   409→            raise ValueError("Must specify either workspace or docker_image")

   501→def create_environment(
   502→    workspace: str | None = None,
   503→    docker_image: str | None = None,
   504→    pip_packages: Sequence[str] | None = None,
   505→    env_vars: dict[str, str] | None = None,
   506→    extras: Sequence[str] | None = None,
   507→) -> EnvironmentConfig:
   508→    """Create an EnvironmentConfig
   509→
   510→    By default, sets the following environment variables:
   511→    - HF_DATASETS_TRUST_REMOTE_CODE: "1" (allows custom dataset code)
   512→    - TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM: "false" (avoids tokenizer deadlocks)
   513→    - HF_TOKEN
   514→    - WANDB_API_KEY
   515→
   516→    Args:
   517→        workspace: Path to workspace root (default: current directory)
   518→        docker_image: Docker image (mutually exclusive with workspace)
   519→        pip_packages: Additional pip packages to install
   520→        env_vars: Custom environment variables (merged with defaults)
   521→        extras: Extra dependency groups for uv
   522→
   523→    Returns:
   524→        EnvironmentConfig with defaults applied
   525→    """
   526→
   527→    if workspace is None and docker_image is None:
   528→        workspace = os.getcwd()
   529→
   530→    default_env_vars = {
   531→        "HF_DATASETS_TRUST_REMOTE_CODE": "1",
   532→        "TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM": "false",
   533→        "HF_TOKEN": os.getenv("HF_TOKEN"),
   534→        "WANDB_API_KEY": os.getenv("WANDB_API_KEY"),
   535→        "MARIN_CI_DISABLE_RUNTIME_ENVS": os.getenv("MARIN_CI_DISABLE_RUNTIME_ENVS"),
   536→    }
   537→
   538→    # Filter out None values - Ray requires all env var values to be strings
   539→    merged_env_vars = {k: v for k, v in {**default_env_vars, **(env_vars or {})}.items() if v is not None}
   540→
   541→    return EnvironmentConfig(
   542→        workspace=workspace,
   543→        docker_image=docker_image,
   544→        pip_packages=list(pip_packages or []),
   545→        env_vars=merged_env_vars,
   546→        extras=list(extras or []),
   547→    )
   548→
   549→
   550→class Cluster(ABC):
   551→    """Abstract interface for cluster job scheduling."""
   552→
   553→    @abstractmethod
   554→    def launch(self, request: JobRequest) -> JobId:
   555→        """Launch a job on the cluster.
   556→
   557→        Args:
   558→            request: Job specification including resources, environment, and entrypoint
   559→
   560→        Returns:
   561→            Unique identifier for the launched job
   562→
   563→        Raises:
   564→            ValueError: If the request is invalid
   565→            RuntimeError: If job submission fails
   566→        """
   567→        ...
   568→
   569→    @abstractmethod
   570→    def monitor(self, job_id: JobId) -> JobInfo:
   571→        """Stream logs from a running job, blocking until completion.
   572→
   573→        Logs are emitted directly via the logger. Blocks until the job
   574→        completes or is terminated.
   575→
   576→        Args:
   577→            job_id: Job identifier returned by launch()
   578→
   579→        Returns:
   580→            JobInfo with final job status
   581→
   582→        Raises:
   583→            KeyError: If job_id is not found
   584→        """
   585→        ...
   586→
   587→    @abstractmethod
   588→    def poll(self, job_id: JobId) -> JobInfo:
   589→        """Get current status of a job without blocking.
   590→
   591→        Args:
   592→            job_id: Job identifier
   593→
   594→        Returns:
   595→            Current job information including status
   596→
   597→        Raises:
   598→            KeyError: If job_id is not found
   599→        """
   600→        ...
   601→
   602→    @abstractmethod
   603→    def terminate(self, job_id: JobId) -> None:
   604→        """Terminate a running job.
   605→
   606→        Attempts graceful termination first, then forceful kill if needed.
   607→
   608→        Args:
   609→            job_id: Job identifier
   610→
   611→        Raises:
   612→            KeyError: If job_id is not found
   613→        """
   614→        ...
   615→
   616→    @abstractmethod
   617→    def list_jobs(self) -> list[JobInfo]:
   618→        """List all jobs managed by this cluster.
   619→
   620→        Returns:
   621→            List of job information for all jobs (running, completed, and failed)
   622→        """
   623→        ...
   624→
   625→    def wait(self, job_id: JobId | Sequence[JobId], raise_on_failure: bool = False) -> JobInfo | list[JobInfo]:
   626→        """Block until the specified job(s) complete, returning final status.
   627→
   628→        Args:
   629→            job_id: Single job ID or sequence of job IDs to wait for
   630→            raise_on_failure: If True, raises RuntimeError when any job fails
   631→
   632→        Returns:
   633→            JobInfo for single job, or list of JobInfo for multiple jobs
   634→        """
   635→        if isinstance(job_id, str):
   636→            return self._wait_single(job_id, raise_on_failure)
   637→
   638→        # Multiple jobs: wait for all, then check for failures
   639→        results = [self._wait_single(jid, raise_on_failure=False) for jid in job_id]
   640→        if raise_on_failure:
   641→            failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (JobStatus.FAILED, JobStatus.STOPPED)]
   642→            if failed:
   643→                msg = "; ".join(f"{r.job_id}: {r.error_message}" for r in failed)
   644→                raise RuntimeError(f"{len(failed)} job(s) failed: {msg}")
   645→        return results
   646→
   647→    def _wait_single(self, job_id: JobId, raise_on_failure: bool = False) -> JobInfo:
   648→        """Wait for a single job to complete."""
   649→        logger.info(f"Starting wait for job {job_id}")
   650→        sleep_secs = 0.1
   651→        max_sleep_secs = 10.0
   652→
   653→        while True:
   654→            info = self.poll(job_id)
   655→            if JobStatus.finished(info.status):
   656→                logger.info(f"Job {job_id} completed with status {info.status}")
   657→                if raise_on_failure and info.status in (JobStatus.FAILED, JobStatus.STOPPED):
   658→                    raise RuntimeError(f"Job {job_id} failed with status {info.status} and error: {info.error_message}")
   659→                return info
   660→            time.sleep(sleep_secs)
   661→            sleep_secs = min(sleep_secs * 2, max_sleep_secs)
   662→

    60→class Evaluator(ABC):
    61→    def _get_scheduling_strategy(self, resource_config: ResourceConfig | None):
    62→        if resource_config is None:
    63→            return None
    64→        return get_scheduling_strategy(resource_config)
    65→
    66→    @abstractmethod
    67→    def launch_evaluate_with_ray(
    68→        self,
    69→        model: ModelConfig,
    70→        evals: list[EvalTaskConfig],
    71→        output_path: str,
    72→        resource_config: ResourceConfig,
    73→        max_eval_instances: int | None = None,
    74→        wandb_tags: list[str] | None = None,
    75→    ) -> None:
    76→        """
    77→        Launches the evaluation run with Ray.
    78→
    79→        Args:
    80→            model (ModelConfig): The model configuration of the model we want to evaluate
    81→            evals (List[EvalTaskConfig]): The list of evaluations to run.
    82→            output_path (str): The path to save the evaluation results.
    83→            max_eval_instances (int | None): The maximum number of evaluation instances to run.
    84→            step (ExecutorStep | None): The step to evaluate. Used to get the config for the model and the trainer.
    85→            wandb_tags (list[str] | None): The tags to add to the wandb run.
    86→        """
    87→        pass
    88→
    89→    @abstractmethod
    90→    def evaluate(
    91→        self,
    92→        model: ModelConfig,
    93→        evals: list[EvalTaskConfig],
    94→        output_path: str,
    95→        max_eval_instances: int | None = None,
    96→        wandb_tags: list[str] | None = None,
    97→    ) -> None:
    98→        """What to run to evaluate."""
    99→        pass
   100→
   101→
   102→def launch_evaluate_with_ray(
   103→    *,
   104→    evaluator: Evaluator,
   105→    job_name: str,
   106→    model: ModelConfig,
   107→    evals: list[EvalTaskConfig],
   108→    output_path: str,
   109→    resource_config: ResourceConfig,
   110→    max_eval_instances: int | None = None,
   111→    wandb_tags: list[str] | None = None,
   112→    extras: Sequence[str] = (),
   113→    pip_packages: Sequence[str] = (),
   114→    env_vars: dict[str, str] | None = None,
   115→    configure_logging: bool = True,
   116→    max_retries_failure: int = 0,
   117→    max_retries_preemption: int = 100,
   118→) -> None:
   119→    """Launch an evaluator on the Ray/Fray cluster."""
   120→
   121→    def launch(
   122→        model: ModelConfig,
   123→        evals: list[EvalTaskConfig],
   124→        output_path: str,
   125→        max_eval_instances: int | None = None,
   126→        wandb_tags: list[str] | None = None,
   127→    ) -> None:
   128→        if configure_logging:
   129→            import logging
   130→
   131→            _init_logging(level=logging.INFO)
   132→        evaluator.evaluate(model, evals, output_path, max_eval_instances, wandb_tags)
   133→
   134→    def _run() -> None:
   135→        with remove_tpu_lockfile_on_exit():
   136→            launch(model, evals, output_path, max_eval_instances, wandb_tags)
   137→
   138→    if resource_config is None:
   139→        resource_config = ResourceConfig()
   140→
   141→    job_request = JobRequest(
   142→        name=job_name,
   143→        entrypoint=Entrypoint.from_callable(_run),
   144→        resources=resource_config,
   145→        environment=EnvironmentConfig.create(
   146→            extras=list(extras),
   147→            pip_packages=list(pip_packages),
   148→            env_vars=env_vars,
   149→        ),
   150→        max_retries_failure=max_retries_failure,
   151→        max_retries_preemption=max_retries_preemption,
   152→    )
   153→
   154→    cluster = current_cluster()
   155→    job_id = cluster.launch(job_request)
   156→    cluster.wait(job_id, raise_on_failure=True)

    13→
    14→Usage (Iris with Daytona):
    15→    uv run iris --config lib/iris/examples/marin.yaml job run \
    16→        --tpu v5p-8 \
    17→        --extra harbor --extra vllm \
    18→        -e MARIN_PREFIX gs://marin-us-central1 \
    19→        -e DAYTONA_API_KEY ${DAYTONA_API_KEY} \
    20→        -e WANDB_API_KEY ${WANDB_API_KEY} \
    21→        -e WANDB_ENTITY marin-community \
    22→        -e WANDB_PROJECT harbor \
    23→        -e HF_TOKEN ${HF_TOKEN} \
    24→        --no-wait \
    25→        -- python experiments/exp3490_nemotron_terminal_8b_tb.py
    26→
    27→    For TBLite (open-thoughts/OpenThoughts-TBLite):
    28→        Add -e HARBOR_DATASET_LITE true to the command above.
    29→
    30→    To evaluate a custom checkpoint:
    31→        Add -e HARBOR_MODEL_NAME <name> -e HARBOR_MODEL_PATH <gs://path/to/hf/checkpoint/>.
    32→
    33→Environment variables:
    34→    - ENV_TYPE: "local" | "daytona" | "e2b" | "modal" (default: "daytona")
    35→    - HARBOR_DATASET_LITE: "true" | "1" to use TBLite instead of TB2 (default: false)
    36→    - HARBOR_MAX_INSTANCES: number of tasks to run (default: all)
    37→    - HARBOR_N_CONCURRENT: number of parallel trials (default: 100)
    38→    - HARBOR_AGENT_KWARGS_JSON: JSON dict forwarded to Harbor AgentConfig.kwargs
    39→"""
    40→

    50→    level=logging.INFO,

    52→)
    53→
    54→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    55→
    56→
    57→def _optional_int_from_env(var_name: str, *, default: int | None) -> int | None:
    58→    value = os.environ.get(var_name)
    59→    if value is None:
    60→        return default
    61→
    62→    if value.strip().lower() in {"none", "null", "all"}:
    63→        return None
    64→
    65→    return int(value)
    66→
    67→
    68→def _optional_json_dict_from_env(var_name: str) -> dict:
    69→    value = os.environ.get(var_name)
    70→    if value is None or not value.strip():
    71→        return {}
    72→
    73→    parsed = json.loads(value)
    74→    if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
    75→        raise TypeError(f"{var_name} must be a JSON object (dict), got {type(parsed)}")
    76→    return parsed
    77→
    78→
    79→USE_TBLITE = os.environ.get("HARBOR_DATASET_LITE", "").strip().lower() in {"true", "1", "yes"}
    80→
    81→if USE_TBLITE:
    82→    DATASET = "open-thoughts/OpenThoughts-TBLite"
    83→    VERSION = "hf"
    84→else:
    85→    DATASET = "terminal-bench"
    86→    VERSION = "2.0"
    87→
    88→ENV_TYPE = os.environ.get("ENV_TYPE", "daytona")
    89→HARBOR_AGENT = "terminus-2"
    90→
    91→MODEL_NAME = os.environ.get("HARBOR_MODEL_NAME", "nemotron-terminal-8b")
    92→MODEL_PATH = os.environ.get("HARBOR_MODEL_PATH", "gs://marin-us-central1/models/nvidia--Nemotron-Terminal-8B--main/")
    93→
    94→HARBOR_MAX_INSTANCES = _optional_int_from_env("HARBOR_MAX_INSTANCES", default=None)
    95→HARBOR_N_CONCURRENT = _optional_int_from_env("HARBOR_N_CONCURRENT", default=25) or 1
    96→HARBOR_AGENT_KWARGS = _optional_json_dict_from_env("HARBOR_AGENT_KWARGS_JSON")
    97→
    98→# Sampling parameters from the model's generation_config.json to match the paper.
    99→# Terminus-2 defaults to temperature=0.7; the model was evaluated with temperature=0.6.
   100→HARBOR_AGENT_KWARGS.setdefault("temperature", 0.6)
   101→
   102→DATASET_PATH_SEGMENT = DATASET.replace("/", "__") if USE_TBLITE else "terminal-bench"
   103→OUTPUT_DIR = os.path.join("evaluation", "harbor", DATASET_PATH_SEGMENT, MODEL_NAME, HARBOR_AGENT)
   104→
   105→if ENV_TYPE == "daytona" and not os.environ.get("DAYTONA_API_KEY"):
   106→    logger.warning(
   107→        "DAYTONA_API_KEY not set but ENV_TYPE=daytona! "
   108→        "The evaluation will likely fail. Set DAYTONA_API_KEY or use ENV_TYPE=local for Docker."
   109→    )
   110→
   111→logger.info("=" * 80)
   112→logger.info("Reproduce NemotronTerminal-8B on Terminal-Bench 2.0")
   113→logger.info("=" * 80)
   114→logger.info("Dataset: %s@%s", DATASET, VERSION)
   115→logger.info("Model: %s", MODEL_NAME)
   116→logger.info("Model path: %s", MODEL_PATH)
   117→logger.info("Agent: %s", HARBOR_AGENT)
   118→logger.info("Env: %s", ENV_TYPE)
   119→logger.info("Max tasks: %s", HARBOR_MAX_INSTANCES)
   120→logger.info("Concurrent trials: %s", HARBOR_N_CONCURRENT)
   121→logger.info("Output dir (under MARIN_PREFIX): %s", OUTPUT_DIR)
   122→logger.info("=" * 80)
   123→
   124→
   125→if __name__ == "__main__":
   126→    step = evaluate_harbor(
   127→        model_name=MODEL_NAME,
   128→        model_path=MODEL_PATH,
   129→        dataset=DATASET,
   130→        version=VERSION,
   131→        REDACTED,
   132→        resource_config=ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v5p-8"),
   133→        wandb_tags=["harbor", DATASET, "terminus-2", ENV_TYPE, MODEL_NAME, "vllm", "repro-3490"]
   134→        + (["tblite"] if USE_TBLITE else []),
   135→        agent=HARBOR_AGENT,
   136→        n_concurrent=HARBOR_N_CONCURRENT,
   137→        env=ENV_TYPE,
   138→        agent_kwargs=HARBOR_AGENT_KWARGS,
   139→    ).with_output_path(OUTPUT_DIR)
   140→
   141→    executor_main(steps=[step])

   100→
   101→    Always appends /hf for ExecutorSteps; run.py's _normalize_model_path handles
   102→    detecting whether the HF files are at root or in /hf at evaluation time.
   103→    """
   104→    if isinstance(step, ExecutorStep):
   105→        model_step_path = output_path_of(step, "hf")
   106→        name = step.name
   107→    elif isinstance(step, InputName):
   108→        # `InputName.hardcoded(...)` has `step.step is None`; treat it as a direct path.
   109→        if step.step is None:
   110→            if step.name is None:
   111→                raise ValueError("Invalid InputName: both `step` and `name` are None.")
   112→            model_step_path = step.name
   113→            name = _infer_model_name_for_path(step.name)
   114→        else:
   115→            # If `name` is already set, the InputName refers to a specific subpath under the step's output.
   116→            # Otherwise default to the HF export directory.
   117→            model_step_path = step if step.name is not None else output_path_of(step.step, "hf")
   118→            name = step.step.name
   119→    elif isinstance(step, str):
   120→        model_step_path = step
   121→        name = _infer_model_name_for_path(step)
   122→    else:
   123→        raise ValueError(f"Invalid step type: {step}")
   124→
   125→    return name, model_step_path
   126→
   127→

   158→def default_eval(
   159→    step: ExecutorStep | InputName | str,
   160→    resource_config: ResourceConfig = ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v4-8"),
   161→    evals: list[EvalTaskConfig] | None = None,
   162→    max_eval_instances: int | None = None,
   163→    apply_chat_template: bool = False,
   164→    discover_latest_checkpoint: bool = True,
   165→) -> ExecutorStep:
   166→    """
   167→    Create an ExecutorStep to evaluate the model using LM Evaluation Harness on a step.
   168→
   169→    Args:
   170→        step (ExecutorStep | InputName): step to evaluate.
   171→        evals (list[EvalTaskConfig]): List of evals to run- defaults to a set of CORE_TASKS defined in task_configs.py
   172→        max_eval_instances (int): Maximum number of evaluation instances to run.
   173→    """
   174→
   175→    # this logic extracts the `ExecutorStep` corresponding to the training step, and get the model path
   176→    name, model_step_path = extract_model_name_and_path(step)
   177→
   178→    logger.info(f"Creating default evaluation step for {name}")
   179→
   180→    # Default to CORE_TASKS
   181→    if evals is None:
   182→        evals = CORE_TASKS
   183→
   184→    logger.info(f"Running evals on the following tasks: {evals}")
   185→
   186→    return evaluate_levanter_lm_evaluation_harness(
   187→        name,
   188→        model_step_path,
   189→        evals,
   190→        resource_config,
   191→        max_eval_instances=max_eval_instances,
   192→        apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   193→        discover_latest_checkpoint=discover_latest_checkpoint,
   194→    )
   195→
   196→
   197→def default_base_eval(
   198→    step: ExecutorStep | InputName | str,
   199→    resource_config: ResourceConfig = ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v6e-8"),
   200→    max_eval_instances: int | None = None,
   201→    engine_kwargs: dict | None = DEFAULT_LM_EVAL_MODEL_KWARGS,
   202→    run_generation_evals: bool = True,
   203→    discover_latest_checkpoint: bool = True,
   204→):
   205→    # Add GPQA to CORE_TASKS
   206→    # Set up evaluations for core tasks (including GPQA)
   207→    eval_jobs = []
   208→    core_grouped = default_eval(
   209→        step=step,
   210→        resource_config=resource_config,
   211→        evals=CORE_TASKS_PLUS_LEADERBOARD,
   212→        discover_latest_checkpoint=discover_latest_checkpoint,
   213→    )
   214→    eval_jobs.append(core_grouped)
   215→
   216→    # Run tasks where we report Macro_Avg separately to make sure the macro avg gets computed correctly.
   217→    mmlu_0shot = default_eval(
   218→        step=step,
   219→        resource_config=resource_config,
   220→        evals=(MMLU_0_SHOT,),
   221→        discover_latest_checkpoint=discover_latest_checkpoint,
   222→    )
   223→    eval_jobs.append(mmlu_0shot)
   224→
   225→    mmlu_5shot = default_eval(
   226→        step=step,
   227→        resource_config=resource_config,
   228→        evals=(MMLU_5_SHOT,),
   229→        discover_latest_checkpoint=discover_latest_checkpoint,
   230→    )
   231→    eval_jobs.append(mmlu_5shot)
   232→
   233→    mmlu_pro_5shot = default_eval(
   234→        step=step,
   235→        resource_config=resource_config,
   236→        evals=(MMLU_PRO_5_SHOT,),
   237→        discover_latest_checkpoint=discover_latest_checkpoint,
   238→    )
   239→    eval_jobs.append(mmlu_pro_5shot)
   240→
   241→    name, model_step_path = extract_model_name_and_path(step)
   242→    if run_generation_evals:
   243→        generation = evaluate_lm_evaluation_harness(
   244→            name,
   245→            model_step_path,
   246→            BASE_GENERATION_TASKS,
   247→            max_eval_instances=max_eval_instances,
   248→            engine_kwargs=engine_kwargs,
   249→            resource_config=resource_config,
   250→            discover_latest_checkpoint=discover_latest_checkpoint,
   251→        )
   252→
   253→        eval_jobs.append(generation)
   254→    return eval_jobs
   255→
   256→
   257→def default_sft_eval(
   258→    step: ExecutorStep | InputName | str,
   259→    resource_config: ResourceConfig = ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v6e-8"),
   260→    max_eval_instances: int | None = None,
   261→    engine_kwargs: dict | None = DEFAULT_LM_EVAL_MODEL_KWARGS,
   262→    run_generation_evals: bool = True,
   263→    apply_chat_template: bool = True,
   264→    use_levanter_inference: bool = False,
   265→):
   266→    # Set up evaluations for core tasks (including GPQA)
   267→    eval_jobs = []
   268→    leaderboard_grouped = default_eval(
   269→        step=step,
   270→        resource_config=resource_config,
   271→        evals=OPEN_LM_LEADERBOARD_MCQ,
   272→        apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   273→    )
   274→    eval_jobs.append(leaderboard_grouped)
   275→
   276→    # Run tasks where we report Macro_Avg separately to make sure the macro avg gets computed correctly.
   277→
   278→    mmlu_5shot = default_eval(
   279→        step=step, resource_config=resource_config, evals=(MMLU_5_SHOT,), apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template
   280→    )
   281→    eval_jobs.append(mmlu_5shot)
   282→
   283→    mmlu_pro_5shot = default_eval(
   284→        step=step, resource_config=resource_config, evals=(MMLU_PRO_5_SHOT,), apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template
   285→    )
   286→    eval_jobs.append(mmlu_pro_5shot)
   287→
   288→    name, model_step_path = extract_model_name_and_path(step)
   289→    if run_generation_evals:
   290→        if use_levanter_inference:
   291→            leaderboard_generation = evaluate_levanter_lm_evaluation_harness(
   292→                name,
   293→                model_step_path,
   294→                KEY_GENERATION_TASKS,
   295→                resource_config,
   296→                max_eval_instances=max_eval_instances,
   297→                apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   298→            )
   299→            eval_jobs.append(leaderboard_generation)
   300→
   301→            olmo_generation = evaluate_levanter_lm_evaluation_harness(
   302→                name,
   303→                model_step_path,
   304→                OPEN_LM_LEADERBOARD_GEN,
   305→                resource_config,
   306→                max_eval_instances=max_eval_instances,
   307→                apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   308→            )
   309→            eval_jobs.append(olmo_generation)
   310→        else:
   311→            leaderboard_generation = evaluate_lm_evaluation_harness(
   312→                name,
   313→                model_step_path,
   314→                KEY_GENERATION_TASKS,
   315→                max_eval_instances=max_eval_instances,
   316→                engine_kwargs=engine_kwargs,
   317→                resource_config=resource_config,
   318→                apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   319→            )
   320→
   321→            eval_jobs.append(leaderboard_generation)
   322→
   323→            olmo_generation = evaluate_lm_evaluation_harness(
   324→                name,
   325→                model_step_path,
   326→                OPEN_LM_LEADERBOARD_GEN,
   327→                max_eval_instances=max_eval_instances,
   328→                engine_kwargs=engine_kwargs,
   329→                resource_config=resource_config,
   330→                apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   331→            )
   332→            eval_jobs.append(olmo_generation)
   333→    return eval_jobs

   336→def default_key_evals(
   337→    step: ExecutorStep | InputName | str,
   338→    resource_config: ResourceConfig,
   339→    model_name: str | None = None,

   348→    if model_name is None:

   373→    ]
