   376→def evaluate_harbor(
   377→    model_name: str,
   378→    model_path: str | None,
   379→    dataset: str,
   380→    version: str = "1.0",
   381→    max_eval_instances: int | None = None,
   382→    resource_config: ResourceConfig | None = None,
   383→    apply_chat_template: bool = False,
   384→    wandb_tags: list[str] | None = None,
   385→    generation_params: dict | None = None,
   386→    agent: str = "claude-code",
   387→    n_concurrent: int = 4,
   388→    env: str = "local",
   389→    agent_kwargs: dict | None = None,
   390→) -> ExecutorStep:
   391→    """
   392→    Evaluate on ANY Harbor dataset from the registry.
   393→
   394→    No custom adapters needed! Harbor's registry handles all datasets generically.
   395→
   396→    Available datasets: https://harborframework.com/registry
   397→    - aime@1.0: 60 math problems (AIME 2024, 2025-I, 2025-II)
   398→    - terminal-bench@2.0: 89 terminal tasks
   399→    - swebench-verified@1.0: 500 software engineering tasks
   400→    - And 40+ more benchmarks!
   401→
   402→    Args:
   403→        model_name: Model identifier
   404→        model_path: Path to model (can be None for API models like Claude)
   405→        dataset: Harbor dataset name (e.g., "aime", "terminal-bench", "swebench-verified")
   406→        version: Dataset version (e.g., "1.0", "2.0")
   407→        max_eval_instances: Limit number of tasks to run
   408→        resource_config: Resource configuration for Ray
   409→        apply_chat_template: Whether to apply chat template (not used by Harbor)
   410→        wandb_tags: Tags for W&B logging
   411→        generation_params: Generation parameters (not used by Harbor)
   412→        agent: Harbor agent type ("claude-code", "terminus-2", etc.)
   413→        n_concurrent: Number of parallel trials
   414→        env: Environment type ("local", "daytona", "e2b", "modal")
   415→
   416→    Returns:
   417→        ExecutorStep configured for Harbor evaluation
   418→
   419→    Examples:
   420→        # AIME evaluation
   421→        evaluate_harbor("claude-opus-4", None, "aime", "1.0")
   422→
   423→        # Terminal-Bench
   424→        evaluate_harbor("qwen2.5-7b", "gs://.../model", "terminal-bench", "2.0")
   425→
   426→        # SWE-bench Verified
   427→        evaluate_harbor("claude-opus-4", None, "swebench-verified", "1.0", max_eval_instances=10)
   428→    """
   429→
   430→    # Harbor config goes in engine_kwargs
   431→    # Forward env vars through engine_kwargs so they survive serialization across
   432→    # fray job boundaries (Ray runtime env vars don't propagate to nested sub-jobs).
   433→    forwarded_env_vars = _env_vars_from_keys(HARBOR_FORWARDED_ENV_KEYS)
   434→    engine_kwargs = {
   435→        "harbor_config": {
   436→            "dataset": dataset,
   437→            "version": version,
   438→            "agent": agent,
   439→            "n_concurrent": n_concurrent,
   440→            "env": env,
   441→            "agent_kwargs": agent_kwargs or {},
   442→        },
   443→        "forwarded_env_vars": forwarded_env_vars,
   444→    }
   445→
   446→    # When model_path is set, the evaluator launches a fray sub-job for vLLM serving
   447→    # with the correct resources. The outer executor step runs on CPU.
   448→    dispatch_resources = ResourceConfig.with_cpu() if model_path else resource_config
   449→    return ExecutorStep(
   450→        name=f"evaluation/harbor/{model_name}-{dataset}-{version}",
   451→        fn=remote(evaluate, resources=dispatch_resources, pip_dependency_groups=["harbor"]),
   452→        config=EvaluationConfig(
   453→            evaluator="harbor",
   454→            model_name=model_name,
   455→            model_path=model_path,
   456→            evaluation_path=this_output_path(),
   457→            evals=[],  # Harbor uses dataset directly, not evals
   458→            max_eval_instances=max_eval_instances,
   459→            launch_with_ray=True,
   460→            discover_latest_checkpoint=False,
   461→            engine_kwargs=engine_kwargs,
   462→            resource_config=resource_config,
   463→            apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   464→            wandb_tags=wandb_tags,
   465→            generation_params=generation_params,
   466→        ),
   467→    )
   468→
   469→
   470→def evaluate_evalchemy(
   471→    model_name: str,
   472→    model_path: str,
   473→    evals: Sequence[EvalTaskConfig],
   474→    max_eval_instances: int | None = None,
   475→    engine_kwargs: dict | None = None,
   476→    generation_params: dict | None = None,
   477→    resource_config: ResourceConfig | None = None,
   478→    apply_chat_template: bool = False,
   479→    wandb_tags: list[str] | None = None,
   480→    discover_latest_checkpoint: bool = True,
   481→    base_eval_run_name: str | None = None,
   482→) -> ExecutorStep:
   483→    """
   484→    Create an ExecutorStep to evaluate the model using Evalchemy.
   485→
   486→    Args:
   487→        model_name (str): Name of the model.
   488→        model_path (str): Path to the model.
   489→        evals (Sequence[EvalTaskConfig]): Evaluations to run with Evalchemy.
   490→        max_eval_instances (int | None): Maximum number of evaluation instances to run.
   491→        engine_kwargs (dict | None): Additional engine kwargs for vLLM.
   492→        generation_params (dict | None): Generation parameters including:
   493→            - temperature: float (e.g., 0.7)
   494→            - top_p: float (e.g., 1.0)
   495→            - max_gen_toks: int (e.g., 32768)
   496→            - seeds: list[int] for multiple runs with different seeds
   497→        resource_config (ResourceConfig | None): Resource configuration for the job.
   498→        apply_chat_template (bool): Whether to apply chat template.
   499→        wandb_tags (list[str] | None): Tags to add to the WandB run.
   500→        discover_latest_checkpoint (bool): Whether to discover the latest checkpoint.
   501→    """
   502→    # Include task names and seed in the step name to ensure different runs get different output paths
   503→    task_names = "_".join(sorted(e.name for e in evals))
   504→    seed = generation_params.get("seed") if generation_params else None
   505→    seed_suffix = f"_seed{seed}" if seed is not None else ""
   506→    return ExecutorStep(
   507→        name=f"evaluation/evalchemy/{model_name}/{task_names}{seed_suffix}",
   508→        fn=evaluate,
   509→        config=EvaluationConfig(
   510→            evaluator="evalchemy",
   511→            model_name=model_name,
   512→            model_path=model_path,
   513→            evaluation_path=this_output_path(),
   514→            evals=evals,
   515→            max_eval_instances=max_eval_instances,
   516→            launch_with_ray=True,
   517→            discover_latest_checkpoint=discover_latest_checkpoint,
   518→            engine_kwargs=engine_kwargs,
   519→            generation_params=generation_params,
   520→            resource_config=resource_config,
   521→            apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   522→            wandb_tags=wandb_tags,
   523→            base_eval_run_name=base_eval_run_name,
   524→        ),
   525→    )
   526→
   527→
   528→def default_evalchemy_eval(
   529→    step: ExecutorStep | InputName | str,
   530→    resource_config: ResourceConfig = ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v5p-8"),
   531→    evals: Sequence[EvalTaskConfig] | None = None,
   532→    max_eval_instances: int | None = None,
   533→    engine_kwargs: dict | None = None,
   534→    generation_params: dict | None = None,
   535→    apply_chat_template: bool = False,
   536→    discover_latest_checkpoint: bool = True,
   537→    base_eval_run_name: str | None = None,
   538→) -> ExecutorStep:
   539→    """
   540→    Create an ExecutorStep to evaluate the model using Evalchemy reasoning benchmarks.
   541→
   542→    Args:
   543→        step (ExecutorStep | InputName | str): Step to evaluate.
   544→        resource_config (ResourceConfig): Resource configuration (defaults to v5p-8 TPU).
   545→        evals (list[EvalTaskConfig] | None): List of evals to run. Defaults to EVALCHEMY_CORE_TASKS.
   546→        max_eval_instances (int | None): Maximum number of evaluation instances to run.
   547→        engine_kwargs (dict | None): Additional vLLM engine kwargs (optional for evalchemy).
   548→        generation_params (dict | None): Generation parameters including:
   549→            - temperature: float (e.g., 0.7)
   550→            - top_p: float (e.g., 1.0)
   551→            - max_gen_toks: int (e.g., 32768)
   552→            - seed: int for reproducibility
   553→        apply_chat_template (bool): Whether to apply chat template.
   554→        discover_latest_checkpoint (bool): Whether to discover the latest checkpoint.
   555→    """
   556→    name, model_step_path = extract_model_name_and_path(step)
   557→
   558→    # If base_eval_run_name is provided, use it for the output path name
   559→    if base_eval_run_name:
   560→        # When step is a raw string (e.g. a GCS path), search it directly for a step number.
   561→        # Otherwise, use the extracted name which already incorporates the path structure.
   562→        path_str = step if isinstance(step, str) else name
   563→        step_match = re.search(r"step-(\d+)", path_str)
   564→        step_suffix = f"-step{step_match.group(1)}" if step_match else ""
   565→        name = f"{base_eval_run_name}{step_suffix}"
   566→
   567→    logger.info(f"Creating Evalchemy evaluation step for {name}")
   568→
   569→    if evals is None:
   570→        evals = EVALCHEMY_CORE_TASKS
   571→
   572→    logger.info(f"Running Evalchemy evals on the following tasks: {evals}")
   573→
   574→    return evaluate_evalchemy(
   575→        name,
   576→        model_step_path,
   577→        evals,
   578→        max_eval_instances=max_eval_instances,
   579→        engine_kwargs=engine_kwargs or {},  # Pass empty dict to avoid warning
   580→        generation_params=generation_params,
   581→        resource_config=resource_config,
   582→        apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   583→        discover_latest_checkpoint=discover_latest_checkpoint,
   584→        base_eval_run_name=base_eval_run_name,
   585→    )
   586→
   587→
   588→def compile_evalchemy_results(
   589→    steps: list[ExecutorStep],
   590→    seeds: list[int] | None = None,
   591→    base_eval_run_name: str | None = None,
   592→    model_path: str | None = None,
   593→    task_name: str | None = None,
   594→) -> ExecutorStep:
   595→    """
   596→    Compile results from multiple Evalchemy evaluation steps into aggregated metrics.
   597→
   598→    Takes a list of ExecutorSteps for evalchemy tasks and compiles the results into a
   599→    single DataFrame, then logs averaged results to wandb.
   600→
   601→    Args:
   602→        steps: List of ExecutorSteps from evalchemy evaluations (one per seed).
   603→        seeds: List of seeds used for the evaluations (for wandb config).
   604→
   605→    Returns:
   606→        ExecutorStep that compiles and logs aggregated results.
   607→    """
   608→    # Create input paths from steps
   609→    input_paths = [step.cd("results.json") for step in steps]
   610→    output_path = OutputName("compiled_results")
   611→
   612→    # Build compile step name matching the individual run hierarchy:
   613→    #   individual: evaluation/evalchemy/{model_id}/AIME24_seed42
   614→    #   compiled:   evaluation/evalchemy/{model_id}/compile_AIME24_avg5seeds
   615→    if base_eval_run_name:
   616→        step_match = re.search(r"step-(\d+)", model_path or "")
   617→        step_suffix = f"-step{step_match.group(1)}" if step_match else ""
   618→        model_id = f"{base_eval_run_name}{step_suffix}"
   619→    elif model_path:
   620→        model_id = _infer_model_name_for_path(model_path)
   621→    else:
   622→        model_id = "unknown"
   623→
   624→    num_seeds = len(seeds) if seeds else len(steps)
   625→    task_suffix = f"_{task_name}" if task_name else ""
   626→    compile_step_name = f"evaluation/evalchemy/{model_id}/compile{task_suffix}_avg{num_seeds}seeds"
   627→
   628→    return ExecutorStep(
   629→        name=compile_step_name,
   630→        fn=compile_evalchemy_results_fn,
   631→        config={
   632→            "input_paths": input_paths,
   633→            "output_path": output_path,
   634→            "seeds": seeds or [],
   635→            "base_eval_run_name": base_eval_run_name,
   636→            "model_path": model_path,
   637→            "task_name": task_name,
   638→        },
   639→        description="Compile results from multiple evalchemy evaluation steps",
   640→    )
   641→
   642→
   643→def build_evalchemy_eval_steps(
   644→    checkpoints: dict[str | None, list[str]],
   645→    task_seed_groups: list[tuple[list[EvalTaskConfig], list[int]]],
   646→    base_generation_params: dict,
   647→    resource_config: ResourceConfig,
   648→    engine_kwargs: dict | None = None,
   649→    apply_chat_template: bool = True,
   650→    discover_latest_checkpoint: bool = False,
   651→) -> tuple[list[ExecutorStep], list[ExecutorStep]]:
   652→    """Build evaluation and compilation steps for an evalchemy experiment.
   653→
   654→    Creates one evaluation step per (checkpoint, task, seed) combination, plus
   655→    compilation steps that aggregate results across seeds for each (checkpoint, task).
   656→
   657→    Args:
   658→        checkpoints: Mapping from base_eval_run_name to list of checkpoint paths.
   659→            Use None as key to auto-generate names from paths.
   660→        task_seed_groups: List of (tasks, seeds) tuples. Each task in a group
   661→            is evaluated with all seeds in that group.
   662→        base_generation_params: Generation parameters (temperature, top_p, max_gen_toks)
   663→            shared across all runs. Per-seed params are generated by adding "seed": N.
   664→        resource_config: TPU/GPU resource configuration for each eval job.
   665→        engine_kwargs: vLLM engine kwargs (tensor_parallel_size, max_num_seqs, etc.).
   666→        apply_chat_template: Whether to apply chat template.
   667→        discover_latest_checkpoint: Whether to auto-discover latest checkpoint.
   668→
   669→    Returns:
   670→        Tuple of (eval_steps, compile_steps).
   671→    """
   672→    eval_steps: list[ExecutorStep] = []
   673→    compile_steps: list[ExecutorStep] = []
   674→
   675→    for base_eval_run_name, checkpoint_paths in checkpoints.items():
   676→        for checkpoint in checkpoint_paths:
   677→            task_seed_pairs: list[tuple[EvalTaskConfig, list[int]]] = []
   678→            for tasks, seeds in task_seed_groups:
   679→                task_seed_pairs += [(t, seeds) for t in tasks]
   680→
   681→            for task, seeds in task_seed_pairs:
   682→                task_steps: list[ExecutorStep] = []
   683→                for seed in seeds:
   684→                    generation_params = {**base_generation_params, "seed": seed}
   685→                    step = default_evalchemy_eval(
   686→                        step=checkpoint,
   687→                        resource_config=resource_config,
   688→                        evals=[task],
   689→                        engine_kwargs=engine_kwargs,
   690→                        generation_params=generation_params,
   691→                        apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   692→                        discover_latest_checkpoint=discover_latest_checkpoint,
   693→                        base_eval_run_name=base_eval_run_name,
   694→                    )
   695→                    task_steps.append(step)
   696→                    eval_steps.append(step)
   697→
   698→                if len(seeds) > 1:
   699→                    compile_step = compile_evalchemy_results(
   700→                        task_steps,
   701→                        seeds=seeds,
   702→                        base_eval_run_name=base_eval_run_name,
   703→                        model_path=checkpoint,
   704→                        task_name=task.name,
   705→                    )
   706→                    compile_steps.append(compile_step)
   707→
   708→    return eval_steps, compile_steps
   709→
   710→
   711→def run_evalchemy_experiment(
   712→    checkpoints: dict[str | None, list[str]],
   713→    task_seed_groups: list[tuple[list[EvalTaskConfig], list[int]]],
   714→    base_generation_params: dict,
   715→    resource_config: ResourceConfig,
   716→    engine_kwargs: dict | None = None,
   717→    apply_chat_template: bool = True,
   718→    discover_latest_checkpoint: bool = False,
   719→    max_parallel_jobs: int | None = None,
   720→) -> None:
   721→    """Run a complete evalchemy evaluation experiment.
   722→
   723→    Builds eval and compile steps, then executes them via executor_main
   724→    with optional batching for parallel job limits.
   725→
   726→    Args:
   727→        checkpoints: Mapping from base_eval_run_name to list of checkpoint paths.
   728→        task_seed_groups: List of (tasks, seeds) tuples.
   729→        base_generation_params: Shared generation parameters.
   730→        resource_config: TPU/GPU resource configuration.
   731→        engine_kwargs: vLLM engine kwargs.
   732→        apply_chat_template: Whether to apply chat template.
   733→        discover_latest_checkpoint: Whether to auto-discover latest checkpoint.
   734→        max_parallel_jobs: Maximum eval jobs to run concurrently. None for no limit.
   735→    """
   736→    eval_steps, compile_steps = build_evalchemy_eval_steps(
   737→        checkpoints=checkpoints,
   738→        task_seed_groups=task_seed_groups,
   739→        base_generation_params=base_generation_params,
   740→        resource_config=resource_config,
   741→        engine_kwargs=engine_kwargs,
   742→        apply_chat_template=apply_chat_template,
   743→        discover_latest_checkpoint=discover_latest_checkpoint,
   744→    )
   745→
   746→    # Run eval steps in batches to limit parallelism.
   747→    # Each executor_main call runs up to max_parallel_jobs eval steps concurrently.
   748→    # Already-completed steps are automatically skipped via status files on disk.
   749→    if max_parallel_jobs is not None:
   750→        for i in range(0, len(eval_steps), max_parallel_jobs):
   751→            batch = eval_steps[i : i + max_parallel_jobs]
   752→            executor_main(steps=batch)
   753→    else:
   754→        executor_main(steps=eval_steps)
   755→
   756→    # Run compile steps separately. Their eval-step dependencies have already
   757→    # succeeded, so the executor skips them and only runs the compile steps.
   758→    if compile_steps:
   759→        executor_main(steps=compile_steps)
   760→

     4→"""
     5→Script to run an evaluator on a model checkpoint.
     6→
     7→Usage:
     8→
     9→python3 run.py <Name of evaluator> --model <Path to model or Hugging Face model name> \
    10→--evals <List of evals to run> --output-path <Where to output logs and results>
    11→"""
    12→
    13→import logging
    14→import os
    15→import time
    16→
    17→import draccus
    18→
    19→from fray.cluster import ResourceConfig, TpuConfig, get_tpu_topology
    20→from fray.v1.cluster import ResourceConfig as V1ResourceConfig
    21→from fray.v1.cluster import TpuConfig as V1TpuConfig
    22→
    23→from marin.evaluation.evaluation_config import EvaluationConfig
    24→from marin.evaluation.evaluators.evaluator import Evaluator, ModelConfig
    25→from marin.evaluation.evaluators.evalchemy_evaluator import EvalchemyEvaluator
    26→from marin.evaluation.evaluators.harbor_evaluator import HarborEvaluator
    27→from marin.evaluation.evaluators.levanter_lm_eval_evaluator import LevanterLmEvalEvaluator
    28→from marin.evaluation.evaluators.lm_evaluation_harness_evaluator import LMEvaluationHarnessEvaluator
    29→from marin.evaluation.evaluators.simple_evaluator import SimpleEvaluator
    30→from marin.evaluation.utils import discover_hf_checkpoints
    31→from marin.utils import fsspec_exists
    32→
    33→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    34→
    35→EVALUATORS = {
    36→    "lm_evaluation_harness": LMEvaluationHarnessEvaluator,
    37→    "levanter_lm_evaluation_harness": LevanterLmEvalEvaluator,
    38→    "evalchemy": EvalchemyEvaluator,
    39→    "debug": SimpleEvaluator,
    40→    "harbor": HarborEvaluator,
    41→}
    42→
    43→
    44→def get_evaluator(config: EvaluationConfig) -> Evaluator:
    45→    if config.evaluator not in EVALUATORS:
    46→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown evaluator: {config.evaluator}. Available: {list(EVALUATORS.keys())}")
    47→    return EVALUATORS[config.evaluator]()
    48→
    49→
    50→def _to_v1_resource_config(config: ResourceConfig) -> V1ResourceConfig:
    51→    """Convert a v2 ResourceConfig to v1 for the evaluation subsystem.
    52→
    53→    The key difference is `replicas`: v2 means total VMs (slice_count * vm_count),
    54→    v1 means slice_count. For TPU configs we divide back out; for non-TPU configs
    55→    the value passes through unchanged.
    56→    """
    57→    replicas = config.replicas
    58→    device = config.device
    59→    if isinstance(device, TpuConfig):
    60→        try:
    61→            topo = get_tpu_topology(device.variant)
    62→            replicas = max(1, config.replicas // topo.vm_count)
    63→        except ValueError:
    64→            pass
    65→        device = V1TpuConfig(variant=device.variant, topology=device.topology)
    66→
    67→    return V1ResourceConfig(
    68→        cpu=config.cpu,
    69→        ram=config.ram,
    70→        disk=config.disk,
    71→        device=device,
    72→        replicas=replicas,
    73→        preemptible=config.preemptible,
    74→        regions=config.regions,
    75→    )
    76→
    77→
    78→def evaluate(config: EvaluationConfig) -> None:
    79→    logger.info(f"Running evals with args: {config}")
    80→    evaluator: Evaluator = get_evaluator(config)
    81→
    82→    model: ModelConfig = _impute_model_config(config)
    83→    logger.info(f"Evaluating {model.name} with {config.evals}")
    84→
    85→    start_time: float = time.time()
    86→    if config.launch_with_ray:
    87→        v1_resources = _to_v1_resource_config(config.resource_config)
    88→        evaluator.launch_evaluate_with_ray(
    89→            model,
    90→            evals=config.evals,
    91→            output_path=config.evaluation_path,
    92→            max_eval_instances=config.max_eval_instances,
    93→            resource_config=v1_resources,
    94→            wandb_tags=config.wandb_tags,
    95→        )
    96→    else:
    97→        evaluator.evaluate(
    98→            model,
    99→            evals=config.evals,
   100→            output_path=config.evaluation_path,
   101→            max_eval_instances=config.max_eval_instances,
   102→            wandb_tags=config.wandb_tags,
   103→        )
   104→
   105→    logger.info(f"Done (total time: {time.time() - start_time} seconds)")
   106→
   107→
   108→def _impute_model_config(config):
   109→    # For API models (e.g., Claude, GPT-4), we only need model_name
   110→    if config.model_path is None and config.model_name is None:
   111→        raise ValueError("model_name or model_path must be provided")
   112→
   113→    # Handle API-only models (no local path)
   114→    if config.model_path is None:
   115→        model_path = None
   116→        model_name = config.model_name
   117→    else:
   118→        model_path = _normalize_model_path(config.model_path)
   119→
   120→        if config.discover_latest_checkpoint:
   121→            model_path = discover_hf_checkpoints(model_path)[-1]
   122→
   123→        if config.model_name is None:
   124→            if model_path.endswith("/"):
   125→                model_path = model_path[:-1]
   126→
   127→            last_component = model_path.split("/")[-1]
   128→            if not last_component.startswith("step-"):
   129→                model_name = last_component
   130→            else:
   131→                # Have to impute the model name from the path.
   132→                model_name_parts = model_path.split("/")
   133→                # We're looking for something that looks like a run name and something that looks like a step
   134→                # e.g. $RUN/hf/step-$STEP.
   135→                step_part = model_name_parts[-1]
   136→                step_part = step_part.split("-")[1]
   137→
   138→                # Don't assume there's an hf. Look for a run name, which probably has a - in it.
   139→                for part in reversed(model_name_parts[:-1]):
   140→                    if "-" in part:
   141→                        model_name = part
   142→                        break
   143→                else:
   144→                    # just use the penultimate part
   145→                    model_name = model_name_parts[-2]
   146→
   147→                model_name = f"{model_name}-{step_part}"
   148→        else:
   149→            model_name = config.model_name
   150→    generation_params = {}

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the infrastructure. Let me create a detailed analysis report.

## Summary of Investigation Findings

Based on my thorough exploration of the Harbor evaluation infrastructure, here's my comprehensive analysis of the inference bottlenecks:

### 1. **vLLM Configuration & Startup** (`lib/marin/src/marin/inference/vllm_server.py`)

**Current Setup:**
- Single vLLM instance per evaluation run (one instance on TPU v5p-8)
- Started as Docker sidecar with `--privileged` flag on TPU resources
- vLLM launched with basic configuration (no explicit batch size, max_num_seqs, or tensor parallelism settings)
- Timeout: 3600 seconds (1 hour) for server startup

**Engine kwargs passed to vLLM:**
- `max_model_len`: Can be set via engine_kwargs (respects `VLLM_ALLOW_LONG_MAX_MODEL_LEN` env var)
- `gpu_memory_utilization`: Optional, not currently set for TPU evals
- `load_format`: Set to "runai_streamer" for GCS checkpoint streaming (line 274)

**Key TPU-specific environment variables:**
- `VLLM_TPU_DISABLE_TOPK_TOPP_OPTIMIZATION=1` (disabled by default for Harbor evals)
- `VLLM_TPU_SKIP_PRECOMPILE=1` (skips precompilation cache, line 287)
- `JAX_ENABLE_COMPILATION_CACHE=1` (enabled, cache dir set to GCS prefix)
- `JAX_PERSISTENT_CACHE_MIN_ENTRY_SIZE_BYTES=-1` (aggressive caching)

**Missing Optimizations:**
- No explicit `--max-num-seqs` parameter (default vLLM behavior, no batching control)
- No `--tensor-parallel-size` parameter (runs on single TPU pod only)
- No `--disable-log-stats` (logging overhead not mentioned)
- JAX compilation caching points to GCS prefix, which could add latency

### 2. **Harbor Evaluator Configuration** (`lib/marin/src/marin/evaluation/evaluators/harbor_evaluator.py`)

**Current Limitations:**
- **Single vLLM instance bottleneck** (line 294-312): All 25 concurrent trials served by one server
- **`n_concurrent` parameter** (line 258, 368): Directly maps to Harbor's `n_concurrent_trials`
  - Default: 4 trials, current exp3490: 25 trials (changed from default per line 95)
  - This controls Docker sandbox parallelism, NOT vLLM batching
- **No multi-worker support**: No mechanism to split tasks across multiple vLLM instances
- **Warmup request** (line 73-92): ~9 min JIT compilation cost, happens once at startup

**Resource Config Pattern:**
- Uses `ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v5p-8")` (exp3490 line 132)
- Only v5p-8 is used; no support for larger slices (v5p-16, v5p-32) mentioned
- `replicas` parameter exists in ResourceConfig (line 350) but not utilized for Harbor evals

**Environment Variables Forwarded:**
- 11 env vars forwarded (line 95-111), but no inference tuning params exposed to Harbor config

### 3. **ResourceConfig & TPU Topology** (`lib/fray/src/fray/v1/cluster/base.py`)

**TPU Slice Support:**
- v5p topologies supported: v5p-8 through v5p-12288 (lines 38-49, 203-214)
- **Topology metadata available**: Each TPU type has chip_count, host_count, vm_count, chips_per_vm
- **v5p-8**: 4 chips, 1 host, 1 VM (line 203)
- **v5p-16**: 8 chips, 2 hosts, 2 VMs (line 204)
- **v5p-32**: 16 chips, 4 hosts, 4 VMs (line 205)
- `replicas` parameter could support multi-slice TPU training (line 350)

**Key Limitation:**
- ResourceConfig tracks TPU topology but Harbor evaluator doesn't use multi-slice configurations
- No mechanism to split Harbor trials across multiple v5p-8 slices

### 4. **Context Summarization Bottleneck** (`lib/harbor/src/harbor/agents/terminus_2/terminus_2.py`)

**Configuration:**
- **Proactive summarization threshold**: 8000 free tokens (line 141)
- **Summarization process**: 3-step context summarization (line 640) - 3 separate LLM calls per checkpoint
- **Triggers per task**: Multiple times as agent context grows (8000 token threshold is aggressive)

**Impact:**
- Terminus-2 agent makes 3 LLM calls during each summarization event
- These calls are serialized (line 728 mentions single "summary generation LLM call")
- For 25 concurrent trials, if even a few hit summarization, vLLM queue backs up

**No Optimization Hooks:**
- `enable_summarize=True` (default, line 140) - can't be disabled from Harbor config
- `proactive_summarization_threshold` - not exposed in Harbor agent_kwargs (would need custom integration)

### 5. **Evaluation Flow Architecture** (`lib/marin/src/marin/evaluation/run.py`)

**Ray Integration:**
- Single Ray job for entire evaluation (line 86-95)
- ResourceConfig converted from v2 to v1 at line 50-75
- **Replica handling** (line 57-62): v2 replicas converted back to slice count for v1
- No provision for distributing vLLM across multiple Ray actors/workers

**Executor Pattern:**
- `launch_evaluate_with_ray()` in `evaluator.py` (line 102-157)
- Creates single JobRequest for entire evaluation
- No sub-job creation for multiple vLLM instances

### 6. **Experiment Configuration** (`experiments/exp3490_nemotron_terminal_8b_tb.py`)

**Current Parameters:**
- `HARBOR_N_CONCURRENT = 25` (line 95, changed from default of 4)
- `HARBOR_N_CONCURRENT` → 25 sandboxes trying to use 1 vLLM instance
- **No knobs for vLLM optimization**
- Could expose additional vLLM config via `HARBOR_AGENT_KWARGS_JSON` (line 96) but defaults are hardcoded

**Timing Analysis from your notes:**
- TBLite (100 tasks): ~2.5 hours total
  - ~90 seconds per task on average
  - But single failing task can take 3.3 hours
- TB2 (89 tasks): ~6 hours total
  - ~4 minutes per task on average

---

## Key Bottleneck Findings

### **Problem 1: Single vLLM Instance with High Concurrency**
- **Current**: 1 vLLM instance serving 25 concurrent Docker sandboxes
- **vLLM queuing**: Without explicit `--max-num-seqs` parameter, batching is suboptimal
- **No backpressure**: Sandbox requests queue indefinitely at vLLM

### **Problem 2: Context Summarization Not Tuned**
- **3 LLM calls per summarization checkpoint**: Not exposed as configurable
- **8000 token threshold is aggressive**: Triggers summarization too early
- **No batching of summarization calls**: Each is serial

### **Problem 3: No Multi-Worker/Multi-Slice Support**
- **ResourceConfig supports replicas**: But Harbor evaluator ignores it
- **Could split 100 tasks across 2-4 v5p-8 slices**: Each with separate vLLM + 6-12 sandboxes
- **Current design**: Single-slice only

### **Problem 4: TPU Resource Utilization**
- **No explicit batch size tuning**: vLLM default batching may be suboptimal for TPU
- **No tensor parallelism**: Could use tensor parallelism within a slice for faster inference
- **JAX compilation cache on GCS**: Remote access adds latency (consider local caching)
- **JIT warmup cost**: ~9 minutes is high; precompilation skipped

### **Problem 5: Long Per-Task Timeouts**
- **3.3 hours for single task**: Default timeout_sec = 600 (line 10-23 in task config)
- **But Harbor multiplier or override mechanism**: Not visible in code
- **Failing tasks block concurrent execution**: If waterfall parallelism, one slow task blocks others

---

## Optimization Opportunities (No Code Changes Required)

1. **Increase n_concurrent to 50-100** (if not already): Better amortizes vLLM warmup across more requests
2. **Tune vLLM via engine_kwargs**: Pass `max_num_seqs`, `tensor_parallel_size`, etc.
3. **Disable context summarization**: Set `enable_summarize=false` via agent_kwargs if acceptable for eval
4. **Lower proactive_summarization_threshold**: Trigger earlier to avoid longer context resets
5. **Use v5p-16 or v5p-32 with tensor parallelism**: 2-4x faster inference per request
6. **Split tasks across multiple vLLM instances**: Create 2-4 parallel Ray jobs instead of one serial job

---

## Files to Review for Implementation

| File | Purpose | Key Lines |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `lib/marin/src/marin/evaluation/evaluators/harbor_evaluator.py` | vLLM startup & Harbor orchestration | 73-92, 294-312, 368 |
| `lib/marin/src/marin/inference/vllm_server.py` | vLLM server launch & config | 278-289, 638-676, 747-776 |
| `lib/fray/src/fray/v1/cluster/base.py` | ResourceConfig & TPU topology | 350, 370-372 |
| `lib/marin/src/marin/evaluation/run.py` | Ray/Fray evaluation dispatch | 86-95, 50-75 |
| `experiments/exp3490_nemotron_terminal_8b_tb.py` | Current eval parameters | 94-96, 132-138 |
| `lib/harbor/src/harbor/agents/terminus_2/terminus_2.py` | Context summarization config | 140-173 |

All files are read-accessible from `/home/kevin/marin-harbor/`.

toolUseResult.prompt:
I need to understand the inference bottlenecks in Harbor evaluations on Terminal-Bench. The current setup uses a single vLLM instance on a TPU v5p-8 serving 25 concurrent agent sandboxes. The eval takes ~2.5 hours for TBLite (100 tasks) and ~6 hours for TB2 (89 tasks).

From prior analysis (issue comment), the bottlenecks are:
1. Context summarization (terminus-2 agent does 3 LLM calls per checkpoint, triggers at 8000 free tokens)
2. Network I/O in sandboxes (wget/curl downloads)
3. Long per-task timeouts on failing tasks (up to 3.3 hours for a single task)
4. vLLM JIT warmup (~9 min one-time cost)
5. Single vLLM instance bottleneck with high concurrency

Please investigate:

1. Read `lib/marin/src/marin/evaluation/evaluators/harbor_evaluator.py` - look for:
   - How vLLM is configured and started
   - Whether multiple vLLM instances could be used
   - The `n_concurrent` parameter and how it affects vLLM load
   - Any existing parallelism or multi-worker support

2. Read `lib/marin/src/marin/inference/vllm_server.py` - understand:
   - How vLLM is launched (Docker sidecar vs native)
   - What TPU/GPU resources it uses
   - Configuration options (tensor parallelism, max model length, etc.)
   - Whether it supports running on larger TPU slices (v5p-16, v5p-32)

3. Check if there's support for running harbor evals across multiple TPU workers in parallel (e.g., splitting tasks across workers)

4. Look at the `ResourceConfig` and how it maps to TPU slices - can we use a larger slice for faster per-request inference?

5. Check if there are any vLLM configuration knobs for TPU optimization (batch size, compilation settings, etc.)

Be thorough - read the full files.

- Could expose additional vLLM config via `HARBOR_AGENT_KWARGS_JSON` (line 96) but defaults are hardcoded

**Timing Analysis from your notes:**
- TBLite (100 tasks): ~2.5 hours total
  - ~90 seconds per task on average
  - But single failing task can take 3.3 hours
- TB2 (89 tasks): ~6 hours total
  - ~4 minutes per task on average

---

## Key Bottleneck Findings

### **Problem 1: Single vLLM Instance with High Concurrency**
- **Current**: 1 vLLM instance serving 25 concurrent Docker sandboxes
- **vLLM queuing**: Without explicit `--max-num-seqs` parameter, batching is suboptimal
- **No backpressure**: Sandbox requests queue indefinitely at vLLM

### **Problem 2: Context Summarization Not Tuned**
- **3 LLM calls per summarization checkpoint**: Not exposed as configurable
- **8000 token threshold is aggressive**: Triggers summarization too early
- **No batching of summarization calls**: Each is serial

### **Problem 3: No Multi-Worker/Multi-Slice Support**
- **ResourceConfig supports replicas**: But Harbor evaluator ignores it
- **Could split 100 tasks across 2-4 v5p-8 slices**: Each with separate vLLM + 6-12 sandboxes
- **Current design**: Single-slice only

### **Problem 4: TPU Resource Utilization**
- **No explicit batch size tuning**: vLLM default batching may be suboptimal for TPU
- **No tensor parallelism**: Could use tensor parallelism within a slice for faster inference
- **JAX compilation cache on GCS**: Remote access adds latency (consider local caching)
- **JIT warmup cost**: ~9 minutes is high; precompilation skipped

### **Problem 5: Long Per-Task Timeouts**
- **3.3 hours for single task**: Default timeout_sec = 600 (line 10-23 in task config)
- **But Harbor multiplier or override mechanism**: Not visible in code
- **Failing tasks block concurrent execution**: If waterfall parallelism, one slow task blocks others

---

## Optimization Opportunities (No Code Changes Required)

1. **Increase n_concurrent to 50-100** (if not already): Better amortizes vLLM warmup across more requests
2. **Tune vLLM via engine_kwargs**: Pass `max_num_seqs`, `tensor_parallel_size`, etc.
3. **Disable context summarization**: Set `enable_summarize=false` via agent_kwargs if acceptable for eval
4. **Lower proactive_summarization_threshold**: Trigger earlier to avoid longer context resets
5. **Use v5p-16 or v5p-32 with tensor parallelism**: 2-4x faster inference per request
6. **Split tasks across multiple vLLM instances**: Create 2-4 parallel Ray jobs instead of one serial job

---

## Files to Review for Implementation

| File | Purpose | Key Lines |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `lib/marin/src/marin/evaluation/evaluators/harbor_evaluator.py` | vLLM startup & Harbor orchestration | 73-92, 294-312, 368 |
| `lib/marin/src/marin/inference/vllm_server.py` | vLLM server launch & config | 278-289, 638-676, 747-776 |
| `lib/fray/src/fray/v1/cluster/base.py` | ResourceConfig & TPU topology | 350, 370-372 |
| `lib/marin/src/marin/evaluation/run.py` | Ray/Fray evaluation dispatch | 86-95, 50-75 |
| `experiments/exp3490_nemotron_terminal_8b_tb.py` | Current eval parameters | 94-96, 132-138 |
| `lib/harbor/src/harbor/agents/terminus_2/terminus_2.py` | Context summarization config | 140-173 |

All files are read-accessible from `/home/kevin/marin-harbor/`.

input.content:
# Speeding Up Harbor Terminal-Bench Inference

## Context

TB-Lite takes ~2.5h and TB2 takes ~6h on a single v5p-8. Preemptions mid-run cause full restarts since local trial state is lost. The key bottleneck is a **single vLLM instance on v5p-8 serving 25 concurrent agent sandboxes**.

## Top Opportunities (ordered by impact)

### 1. Use a larger TPU slice (v5p-16 or v5p-32) with tensor parallelism
- **Current**: v5p-8 (4 chips, 1 host) — no tensor parallelism
- **Change**: Use `ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v5p-16")` + pass `tensor_parallel_size=2` via `engine_kwargs`
- **Effect**: ~2x faster per-request inference, better batching throughput
- **Files**: `experiments/exp3490_nemotron_terminal_8b_tb.py` (line 129), `lib/marin/src/marin/inference/vllm_server.py`
- **Risk**: v5p-16 requires 2 VMs (gang scheduling), harder to get allocated, more expensive

### 2. Split tasks across multiple independent v5p-8 workers
- **Current**: 1 job, 1 vLLM, 25 sandboxes
- **Change**: Launch N parallel Ray jobs (e.g., 4 jobs x 25 tasks each) on separate v5p-8 slices
- **Effect**: ~Nx wall-time speedup, each job is smaller so preemption loses less work
- **Files**: `experiments/exp3490_nemotron_terminal_8b_tb.py`, new wrapper script
- **How**: Use `HARBOR_MAX_INSTANCES` env var with task-range splitting, or submit multiple jobs with disjoint task lists
- **Bonus**: Each smaller job finishes faster, reducing preemption exposure

### 3. Tune vLLM batching parameters
- **Current**: Default vLLM batching (no explicit `max_num_seqs`, `max_num_batched_tokens`)
- **Change**: Pass via `engine_kwargs` in `harbor_evaluator.py`:
  - `max_num_seqs=32` (allow more concurrent sequences)
  - `enforce_eager=True` (skip graph compilation overhead on TPU)
- **Files**: `lib/marin/src/marin/inference/vllm_server.py` (line ~278)

### 4. Reduce context summarization overhead
- **Current**: terminus-2 triggers 3 LLM calls per summarization checkpoint at 8000 free tokens
- **Change**: Pass `proactive_summarization_threshold=4000` or `enable_summarize=false` via `agent_kwargs`
- **Effect**: Fewer summarization interruptions = fewer serial LLM round-trips
- **Files**: `experiments/exp3490_nemotron_terminal_8b_tb.py` (agent_kwargs)

### 5. Persist trial results to GCS more aggressively for preemption resilience
- **Current**: Harbor uploads completed trials to GCS, but on preemption the new worker can't resume from GCS (only local disk)
- **Change**: Already implemented in `harbor_evaluator.py` — the resume mechanism checks `/tmp/harbor_workdir/` which is lost on preemption. A GCS-backed resume would be the real fix but requires Harbor library changes.
- **Workaround**: Approach #2 (smaller parallel jobs) naturally reduces per-job preemption cost

## Recommended Immediate Actions

**Quick win (no code changes):** Launch 4 parallel v5p-8 jobs splitting the 100 TBLite tasks into 25 each:
```bash
# Job 1: tasks 0-24
-e HARBOR_MAX_INSTANCES 25 -e HARBOR_TASK_OFFSET 0
# Job 2: tasks 25-49
-e HARBOR_MAX_INSTANCES 25 -e HARBOR_TASK_OFFSET 25
# etc.
```
This requires adding `HARBOR_TASK_OFFSET` support to the experiment file (small code change).

**Medium effort:** Switch to v5p-16 with tensor_parallel_size=2 for ~2x single-stream speedup.

## Verification
- Compare wall time of parallel 4x25 vs serial 1x100
- Compare per-request latency on v5p-8 vs v5p-16

## Context
The TB-Lite evaluation of exp3490b step-1500 completed with 16/100 = 16.0% accuracy. Need to upload results to HF and update the tracking comment on issue #3490.

## Steps

1. **Download eval results from GCS** (skip `harbor_trials/` per feedback memory)

   - Only: `results_*.json`, `samples_*.jsonl`, `trajectories/`

2. **Upload to HuggingFace** as `AlienKevin/nemotron-terminal-8b-25pct-eval-terminal-bench-lite-concurrency-25`

content.text:
Here's what I found — the main bottlenecks and fixes:

**Biggest wins:**

1. **Split tasks across multiple v5p-8 workers in parallel** — Instead of 1 job x 100 tasks, run 4 jobs x 25 tasks on separate v5p-8 slices. Each job finishes ~4x faster, and preemption only loses 1/4 of the work. Requires a small code change to add task offset support.

2. **Use v5p-16 with tensor parallelism** — `tensor_parallel_size=2` on v5p-16 gives ~2x faster per-request inference. Change is just `ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v5p-16")` + engine kwargs.

3. **Tune vLLM batching** — No `max_num_seqs` is currently set, so vLLM uses defaults. Explicitly setting `max_num_seqs=32` improves throughput with 25 concurrent sandboxes.

4. **Reduce context summarization** — terminus-2 does 3 serial LLM calls per summarization checkpoint (triggers at 8000 free tokens). Raising the threshold or disabling summarization cuts a major source of serial inference round-trips.

The **highest-impact, lowest-effort** change is #1 — parallelizing across multiple v5p-8 slices. It also naturally reduces preemption exposure since each job is smaller.

   166→

   168→        logger.addHandler(file_handler)
   169→        self._log_file_handler = file_handler

   173→
   174→        logger.addHandler(console_handler)

   177→        self._logger = logger.getChild(__name__)

   179→    def _close_logger_handlers(self) -> None:
   180→        for handler in (self._log_file_handler, self._console_handler):
   181→            if handler is None:
   182→                continue
   183→            logger.removeHandler(handler)
   184→            handler.close()
   185→
   186→        self._log_file_handler = None
   187→        self._console_handler = None

   253→    def _init_task_configs(self):

   256→        for dataset in self.config.datasets:

   332→    async def _on_trial_completed(self, event: TrialHookEvent) -> None:
   333→        """Internal hook to update job stats when a trial completes."""
   334→        if event.result is None:
   335→            raise ValueError(

   346→            agent_name = trial_result.agent_info.name
   347→            model_name = (

   361→            )

   365→                previous_result=previous_result,

   367→
   368→            self._previous_trial_results[trial_name] = trial_result
   369→
   370→            rewards_list = list(self._live_rewards[evals_key].values())

   376→            await asyncio.to_thread(

   381→    async def run(self) -> JobResult:
   382→        try:
   383→            self._job_result = JobResult(
   384→                id=self._id,
   385→                started_at=datetime.now(),
   386→                n_total_trials=len(self._trial_configs),
   387→                stats=JobStats.from_trial_results(self._existing_trial_results),
   388→            )
   389→
   390→            self._job_config_path.write_text(self.config.model_dump_json(indent=4))
   391→
   392→            trial_results = await self._orchestrator.run()
   393→
   394→            combined_trial_results = self._existing_trial_results + trial_results
   395→
   396→            final_rewards = defaultdict(list)
   397→
   398→            for trial_result in combined_trial_results:
   399→                agent_name = trial_result.agent_info.name
   400→                model_name = (
   401→                    trial_result.agent_info.model_info.name
   402→                    if trial_result.agent_info.model_info
   403→                    else None
   404→                )
   405→                dataset_name = trial_result.source or "adhoc"
   406→                evals_key = JobStats.format_agent_evals_key(
   407→                    agent_name, model_name, dataset_name
   408→                )
   409→
   410→                if trial_result.verifier_result is not None:
   411→                    final_rewards[evals_key].append(
   412→                        trial_result.verifier_result.rewards
   413→                    )
   414→                else:
   415→                    final_rewards[evals_key].append(None)
   416→
   417→            final_stats = JobStats.from_trial_results(combined_trial_results)
   418→
   419→            for evals_key, rewards in final_rewards.items():
   420→                # Extract dataset_name from the evals_key to get the right metrics
   421→                # The key format is either "agent__model__dataset" or "agent__dataset"
   422→                dataset_name = evals_key.split("__")[-1]

   424→                    final_stats.evals[evals_key].metrics.append(metric.compute(rewards))
   425→

   427→
   428→            self._job_result.finished_at = datetime.now()

     9→from harbor.models.metric.config import MetricConfig
    10→from harbor.models.orchestrator_type import OrchestratorType
    11→from harbor.models.registry import LocalRegistryInfo, RemoteRegistryInfo
    12→from harbor.models.task.id import GitTaskId, LocalTaskId

    23→class BaseDatasetConfig(BaseModel, ABC):
    24→    task_names: list[str] | None = Field(
    25→        default=None,
    26→        description="Tasks to include from the dataset. Name can be a glob pattern.",
    27→    )
    28→    exclude_task_names: list[str] | None = Field(
    29→        default=None,
    30→        description="Tasks to exclude from the dataset. Name can be a glob pattern.",
    31→    )
    32→    n_tasks: int | None = Field(
    33→        default=None,
    34→        description="Maximum number of tasks to include from this dataset. Applied after task_names/exclude_task_names filtering.",
    35→    )
    36→
    37→    def _filter_task_ids(
    38→        self, task_ids: list[LocalTaskId | GitTaskId]
    39→    ) -> list[LocalTaskId | GitTaskId]:
    40→        filtered_ids = task_ids
    41→        if self.task_names:
    42→            filtered_ids = [
    43→                task_id
    44→                for task_id in filtered_ids
    45→                if any(
    46→                    fnmatch(task_id.get_name(), pattern_id)
    47→                    for pattern_id in self.task_names
    48→                )
    49→            ]
    50→
    51→        if self.exclude_task_names:
    52→            filtered_ids = [
    53→                task_id
    54→                for task_id in filtered_ids
    55→                if not any(
    56→                    fnmatch(task_id.get_name(), pattern_id)
    57→                    for pattern_id in self.exclude_task_names
    58→                )
    59→            ]
    60→
    61→        if self.n_tasks is not None:
    62→            filtered_ids = filtered_ids[: self.n_tasks]
    63→
    64→        return filtered_ids
    65→
    66→    @abstractmethod
    67→    def get_task_configs(self, disable_verification: bool = False) -> list[TaskConfig]:
    68→        """Get the list of task IDs from this dataset."""
    69→        pass
    70→
    71→
    72→class LocalDatasetConfig(BaseDatasetConfig):
    73→    path: Path
    74→
    75→    def get_task_configs(self, disable_verification: bool = False) -> list[TaskConfig]:
    76→        task_ids: list[LocalTaskId | GitTaskId] = [
    77→            LocalTaskId(path=path)
    78→            for path in self.path.iterdir()
    79→            if TaskPaths(path).is_valid(disable_verification=disable_verification)
    80→        ]
    81→        filtered_task_ids = self._filter_task_ids(task_ids)
    82→        return [
    83→            TaskConfig(path=task_id.path, source=self.path.expanduser().resolve().name)
    84→            for task_id in filtered_task_ids
    85→        ]
    86→
    87→
    88→class RegistryDatasetConfig(BaseDatasetConfig):
    89→    registry: LocalRegistryInfo | RemoteRegistryInfo
    90→    name: str
    91→    version: str | None = None
    92→    overwrite: bool = Field(
    93→        default=False, description="Whether to overwrite cached remote tasks."
    94→    )
    95→    download_dir: Path | None = Field(
    96→        default=None, description="The directory to cache remote tasks to."
    97→    )
    98→
    99→    def get_task_configs(self, disable_verification: bool = False) -> list[TaskConfig]:
   100→        client = RegistryClientFactory.create(self.registry)
   101→
   102→        dataset_spec = client.get_dataset_spec(self.name, self.version)
   103→
   104→        return [
   105→            TaskConfig(
   106→                path=task.path,
   107→                git_url=task.git_url if isinstance(task, GitTaskId) else None,
   108→                git_commit_id=task.git_commit_id
   109→                if isinstance(task, GitTaskId)
   110→                else None,
   111→                overwrite=self.overwrite,
   112→                download_dir=self.download_dir,
   113→                source=self.name,
   114→            )
   115→            for task in self._filter_task_ids(
   116→                [task.to_source_task_id() for task in dataset_spec.tasks]
   117→            )
   118→        ]
   119→
   120→
   121→class RetryConfig(BaseModel):
   122→    max_retries: int = Field(
   123→        default=0, description="Maximum number of retry attempts", ge=0
   124→    )
   125→    include_exceptions: set[str] | None = Field(
   126→        default=None,
   127→        description="Exception types to retry on. If None, retries all exceptions.",
   128→    )
   129→    exclude_exceptions: set[str] | None = Field(
   130→        default_factory=lambda: {
   131→            "AgentTimeoutError",
   132→            "VerifierTimeoutError",
   133→            "RewardFileNotFoundError",
   134→            "RewardFileEmptyError",
   135→            "VerifierOutputParseError",
   136→        },
   137→        description="Exception types to NOT retry on. Takes precedence over "
   138→        "include_exceptions.",
   139→    )
   140→    wait_multiplier: float = Field(
   141→        default=1.0, description="Multiplier for exponential backoff wait time"
   142→    )
   143→    min_wait_sec: float = Field(
   144→        default=1.0, description="Minimum wait time in seconds between retries"
   145→    )
   146→    max_wait_sec: float = Field(
   147→        default=60.0, description="Maximum wait time in seconds between retries"
   148→    )
   149→
   150→
   151→class OrchestratorConfig(BaseModel):
   152→    type: OrchestratorType = OrchestratorType.LOCAL
   153→    n_concurrent_trials: int = 4
   154→    quiet: bool = Field(default=False, description="Suppress trial progress displays")
   155→    retry: RetryConfig = Field(default_factory=RetryConfig)
   156→    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
   157→
   158→
   159→class JobConfig(BaseModel):
   160→    job_name: str = Field(
   161→        default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d__%H-%M-%S")
   162→    )
   163→    jobs_dir: Path = Path("jobs")
   164→    n_attempts: int = 1
   165→    timeout_multiplier: float = 1.0
   166→    debug: bool = Field(default=False, description="Enable debug logging")
   167→    orchestrator: OrchestratorConfig = Field(default_factory=OrchestratorConfig)
   168→    environment: EnvironmentConfig = Field(default_factory=EnvironmentConfig)
   169→    verifier: VerifierConfig = Field(default_factory=VerifierConfig)
   170→    metrics: list[MetricConfig] = Field(default_factory=list)
   171→    agents: list[AgentConfig] = Field(default_factory=lambda: [AgentConfig()])
   172→    datasets: list[LocalDatasetConfig | RegistryDatasetConfig] = Field(
   173→        default_factory=list
   174→    )
   175→    tasks: list[TaskConfig] = Field(default_factory=list)
   176→
   177→    def __eq__(self, other):
   178→        if not isinstance(other, JobConfig):
   179→            return NotImplemented
   180→
   181→        # Exclude job_name from equality comparison
   182→        return (
   183→            self.jobs_dir == other.jobs_dir
   184→            and self.n_attempts == other.n_attempts
   185→            and self.timeout_multiplier == other.timeout_multiplier
   186→            and self.orchestrator == other.orchestrator
   187→            and self.environment == other.environment
   188→            and self.verifier == other.verifier
   189→            and self.metrics == other.metrics
   190→            and self.agents == other.agents
   191→            and self.datasets == other.datasets
   192→            and self.tasks == other.tasks
   193→        )
   194→

1→from collections import defaultdict
     2→from datetime import datetime
     3→from typing import Any
     4→from uuid import UUID
     5→
     6→from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
     7→
     8→from harbor.models.trial.result import TrialResult
     9→
    10→Rewards = dict[str, float | int]
    11→TrialRewardsMap = dict[str, Rewards | None]
    12→EvalsRewardsMap = dict[str, TrialRewardsMap]
    13→
    14→
    15→class AgentDatasetStats(BaseModel):
    16→    n_trials: int = 0
    17→    n_errors: int = 0
    18→    metrics: list[dict[str, Any]] = Field(default_factory=list)
    19→    reward_stats: dict[str, dict[float | int, list[str]]] = Field(
    20→        default_factory=lambda: defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))
    21→    )
    22→    exception_stats: dict[str, list[str]] = Field(
    23→        default_factory=lambda: defaultdict(list)
    24→    )
    25→
    26→
    27→class JobStats(BaseModel):
    28→    n_trials: int = 0
    29→    n_errors: int = 0
    30→    evals: dict[str, AgentDatasetStats] = Field(
    31→        default_factory=lambda: defaultdict(AgentDatasetStats)
    32→    )
    33→
    34→    @staticmethod
    35→    def format_agent_evals_key(
    36→        agent_name: str, model_name: str | None, dataset_name: str
    37→    ) -> str:
    38→        """Format a key for the evals dictionary using agent name, model name, and dataset name."""
    39→        if model_name:
    40→            return f"{agent_name}__{model_name}__{dataset_name}".replace("/", "-")
    41→        return f"{agent_name}__{dataset_name}".replace("/", "-")
    42→
    43→    @classmethod
    44→    def from_trial_results(cls, trial_results: list[TrialResult]) -> "JobStats":
    45→        result = cls()
    46→
    47→        for trial_result in trial_results:
    48→            result.increment(trial_result)
    49→
    50→        return result
    51→
    52→    def increment(self, trial_result: TrialResult) -> None:
    53→        self.n_trials += 1
    54→
    55→        agent_name = trial_result.agent_info.name
    56→        model_name = (
    57→            trial_result.agent_info.model_info.name
    58→            if trial_result.agent_info.model_info
    59→            else None
    60→        )
    61→        dataset_name = trial_result.source or "adhoc"
    62→
    63→        evals_key = self.format_agent_evals_key(agent_name, model_name, dataset_name)
    64→
    65→        if (
    66→            trial_result.verifier_result is not None
    67→            and trial_result.verifier_result.rewards is not None
    68→        ):
    69→            self.evals[evals_key].n_trials += 1
    70→            for key, value in trial_result.verifier_result.rewards.items():
    71→                self.evals[evals_key].reward_stats[key][value].append(
    72→                    trial_result.trial_name
    73→                )
    74→
    75→        if trial_result.exception_info is not None:
    76→            self.evals[evals_key].exception_stats[
    77→                trial_result.exception_info.exception_type
    78→            ].append(trial_result.trial_name)
    79→            self.evals[evals_key].n_errors += 1
    80→            self.n_errors += 1
    81→
    82→    def remove_trial(self, trial_result: TrialResult) -> None:
    83→        """Remove a trial's contributions from stats."""
    84→        self.n_trials -= 1
    85→
    86→        agent_name = trial_result.agent_info.name
    87→        model_name = (
    88→            trial_result.agent_info.model_info.name
    89→            if trial_result.agent_info.model_info
    90→            else None
    91→        )
    92→        dataset_name = trial_result.source or "adhoc"
    93→        evals_key = self.format_agent_evals_key(agent_name, model_name, dataset_name)
    94→
    95→        if (
    96→            trial_result.verifier_result is not None
    97→            and trial_result.verifier_result.rewards is not None
    98→        ):
    99→            self.evals[evals_key].n_trials -= 1
   100→            for key, value in trial_result.verifier_result.rewards.items():
   101→                trial_list = self.evals[evals_key].reward_stats[key][value]
   102→                if trial_result.trial_name in trial_list:
   103→                    trial_list.remove(trial_result.trial_name)
   104→
   105→        if trial_result.exception_info is not None:
   106→            exception_type = trial_result.exception_info.exception_type
   107→            trial_list = self.evals[evals_key].exception_stats[exception_type]
   108→            if trial_result.trial_name in trial_list:
   109→                trial_list.remove(trial_result.trial_name)
   110→            self.evals[evals_key].n_errors -= 1
   111→            self.n_errors -= 1
   112→
   113→    def update_trial(
   114→        self,
   115→        new_result: TrialResult,
   116→        previous_result: TrialResult | None = None,
   117→    ) -> None:
   118→        """Update stats for a trial, removing previous contributions if this is a retry."""
   119→        if previous_result is not None:
   120→            self.remove_trial(previous_result)
   121→        self.increment(new_result)
   122→
   123→
   124→class JobResult(BaseModel):
   125→    id: UUID
   126→    started_at: datetime
   127→    finished_at: datetime | None = None
   128→    n_total_trials: int
   129→    stats: JobStats
   130→    trial_results: list[TrialResult] = Field(default_factory=list)
   131→

1→import asyncio
     2→import logging
     3→import traceback
     4→from datetime import datetime, timezone
     5→from pathlib import Path
     6→from typing import Awaitable, Callable
     7→
     8→from tenacity import (
     9→    retry,
    10→    retry_if_exception_type,
    11→    stop_after_attempt,
    12→    wait_exponential,
    13→)
    14→
    15→from harbor.agents.factory import AgentFactory
    16→from harbor.agents.installed.base import BaseInstalledAgent
    17→from harbor.environments.factory import EnvironmentFactory
    18→from harbor.models.agent.context import AgentContext
    19→from harbor.models.agent.name import AgentName
    20→from harbor.models.task.task import Task
    21→from harbor.models.trial.config import TrialConfig
    22→from harbor.models.trial.paths import EnvironmentPaths, TrialPaths
    23→from harbor.models.trial.result import (
    24→    ExceptionInfo,
    25→    TimingInfo,
    26→    TrialResult,
    27→)
    28→from harbor.tasks.client import TaskClient
    29→from harbor.trial.hooks import TrialEvent, TrialHookEvent
    30→from harbor.utils.logger import logger
    31→from harbor.verifier.verifier import Verifier
    32→
    33→
    34→class AgentSetupTimeoutError(asyncio.TimeoutError):
    35→    pass
    36→
    37→
    38→class AgentTimeoutError(asyncio.TimeoutError):
    39→    pass
    40→
    41→
    42→class VerifierTimeoutError(asyncio.TimeoutError):
    43→    pass
    44→
    45→
    46→class EnvironmentStartTimeoutError(asyncio.TimeoutError):
    47→    pass
    48→
    49→
    50→TrialHookCallback = Callable[[TrialHookEvent], Awaitable[None]]
    51→
    52→
    53→class Trial:
    54→    """
    55→    Runs a trial of a given agent on an environment.
    56→
    57→    1. Initializes the environment.
    58→    2. Runs the agent on the environment.
    59→    3. Verifies the results.
    60→    4. Saves the results.
    61→    5. Cleans up the environment.
    62→    6. Uploads the results.
    63→    """
    64→
    65→    _AGENT_SETUP_TIMEOUT_SEC = 360
    66→
    67→    def __init__(self, config: TrialConfig):
    68→        self.config = config
    69→        self.job_id = config.job_id
    70→        self._are_agent_logs_downloaded = False
    71→
    72→        self._hooks: dict[TrialEvent, list[TrialHookCallback]] = {
    73→            event: [] for event in TrialEvent
    74→        }
    75→
    76→        self._task = self._load_task()
    77→        self._trial_paths = TrialPaths(trial_dir=self.trial_dir)
    78→        self._trial_paths.mkdir()
    79→
    80→        self._log_handler: logging.Handler | None = None
    81→        self._init_logger()
    82→
    83→        self._agent = AgentFactory.create_agent_from_config(
    84→            config.agent,
    85→            logs_dir=self._trial_paths.agent_dir,
    86→            logger=self._logger,
    87→            **(
    88→                {
    89→                    "task_dir": self._task._task_dir,
    90→                    "trial_paths": self._trial_paths,
    91→                }
    92→                if config.agent.name == AgentName.ORACLE.value
    93→                else {}
    94→            ),  # Oracle agent gets special treatment so it can cheat.
    95→        )
    96→
    97→        self._environment = EnvironmentFactory.create_environment_from_config(
    98→            config=config.environment,
    99→            environment_dir=self._task.paths.environment_dir,
   100→            environment_name=self._task.name,
   101→            session_id=self.config.trial_name,
   102→            trial_paths=self._trial_paths,
   103→            task_env_config=self._task.config.environment,
   104→            logger=self._logger,
   105→        )
   106→
   107→        self._agent_timeout_sec = (
   108→            min(
   109→                config.agent.override_timeout_sec
   110→                or self._task.config.agent.timeout_sec,
   111→                config.agent.max_timeout_sec or float("inf"),
   112→            )
   113→            * config.timeout_multiplier
   114→        )
   115→
   116→        self._verifier_timeout_sec = (
   117→            min(
   118→                config.verifier.override_timeout_sec
   119→                or self._task.config.verifier.timeout_sec,
   120→                config.verifier.max_timeout_sec or float("inf"),
   121→            )
   122→            * config.timeout_multiplier
   123→        )
   124→
   125→        self._agent_setup_timeout_sec = (
   126→            config.agent.override_setup_timeout_sec
   127→            if config.agent.override_setup_timeout_sec is not None
   128→            else self._AGENT_SETUP_TIMEOUT_SEC
   129→        ) * self.config.timeout_multiplier
   130→
   131→        self._environment_build_timeout_sec = (
   132→            self._task.config.environment.build_timeout_sec
   133→            * self.config.timeout_multiplier
   134→        )
   135→
   136→        self._result: TrialResult | None = None
   137→
   138→    @property
   139→    def result(self) -> TrialResult:
   140→        if self._result is None:
   141→            raise RuntimeError("Trial result accessed before initialization")
   142→        return self._result
   143→
   144→    def _init_logger(self):
   145→        self._logger = logger.getChild(f"{__name__}.{self.config.trial_name}")
   146→        file_handler = logging.FileHandler(self._trial_paths.log_path)
   147→        file_handler.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
   148→        self._logger.addHandler(file_handler)
   149→        self._log_handler = file_handler
   150→
   151→    def _close_logger_handler(self) -> None:
   152→        if self._log_handler is not None:
   153→            self._logger.removeHandler(self._log_handler)
   154→            self._log_handler.close()
   155→            self._log_handler = None
   156→
   157→    def add_hook(self, event: TrialEvent, hook: TrialHookCallback) -> None:
   158→        """Add an async hook to be called when the specified event occurs."""
   159→        self._hooks[event].append(hook)
   160→
   161→    async def _invoke_hooks(self, event: TrialEvent) -> None:
   162→        """Invoke all hooks registered for the given event."""
   163→        hook_event = TrialHookEvent(
   164→            event=event,
   165→            trial_id=self.config.trial_name,
   166→            task_name=self._task.name,
   167→            config=self.config,
   168→            result=self._result,
   169→        )
   170→        for hook in self._hooks[event]:
   171→            await hook(hook_event)
   172→
   173→    def _load_task(self) -> Task:
   174→        if self.config.task.is_git_task():
   175→            client = TaskClient()
   176→            task_id = self.config.task.get_task_id()
   177→
   178→            task_dir = client.download_tasks(
   179→                task_ids=[task_id],
   180→                overwrite=self.config.task.overwrite,
   181→                output_dir=self.config.task.download_dir,
   182→            )[0]
   183→
   184→            return Task(task_dir=task_dir)
   185→        else:
   186→            return Task(task_dir=self.config.task.path)
   187→
   188→    @property
   189→    def trial_dir(self) -> Path:
   190→        return self.config.trials_dir / self.config.trial_name
   191→
   192→    async def _setup_environment(self) -> None:
   193→        await self._invoke_hooks(TrialEvent.ENVIRONMENT_START)
   194→
   195→        self.result.environment_setup = TimingInfo(
   196→            started_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   197→        )
   198→
   199→        try:
   200→            await self._start_environment_with_retry()
   201→        finally:
   202→            self.result.environment_setup.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   203→
   204→    @retry(
   205→        reraise=True,
   206→        stop=stop_after_attempt(2),
   207→        wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=1, max=10),
   208→        retry=retry_if_exception_type(EnvironmentStartTimeoutError),
   209→    )
   210→    async def _start_environment_with_retry(self) -> None:
   211→        try:
   212→            await asyncio.wait_for(
   213→                self._environment.start(
   214→                    force_build=self.config.environment.force_build
   215→                ),
   216→                timeout=self._environment_build_timeout_sec,
   217→            )
   218→        except asyncio.TimeoutError as e:
   219→            raise EnvironmentStartTimeoutError(
   220→                f"Environment start timed out after {self._environment_build_timeout_sec} seconds"
   221→            ) from e
   222→
   223→    async def _setup_agent(self) -> None:
   224→        self.result.agent_setup = TimingInfo(started_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc))
   225→        try:
   226→            await asyncio.wait_for(
   227→                self._agent.setup(environment=self._environment),
   228→                timeout=self._agent_setup_timeout_sec,
   229→            )
   230→        except asyncio.TimeoutError as e:
   231→            raise AgentSetupTimeoutError(
   232→                f"Agent setup timed out after {self._agent_setup_timeout_sec} seconds"
   233→            ) from e
   234→        finally:
   235→            self.result.agent_setup.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   236→
   237→    async def _execute_agent(self) -> None:
   238→        await self._invoke_hooks(TrialEvent.AGENT_START)
   239→
   240→        self.result.agent_execution = TimingInfo(started_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc))
   241→
   242→        try:
   243→            self.result.agent_result = AgentContext()
   244→
   245→            await asyncio.wait_for(
   246→                self._agent.run(
   247→                    instruction=self._task.instruction,
   248→                    environment=self._environment,
   249→                    context=self.result.agent_result,
   250→                ),
   251→                timeout=self._agent_timeout_sec,
   252→            )
   253→        except asyncio.TimeoutError as e:
   254→            raise AgentTimeoutError(
   255→                f"Agent execution timed out after {self._agent_timeout_sec} seconds"
   256→            ) from e
   257→        finally:
   258→            self.result.agent_execution.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   259→
   260→    async def _run_verification(self) -> None:
   261→        await self._invoke_hooks(TrialEvent.VERIFICATION_START)
   262→
   263→        self.result.verifier = TimingInfo(started_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc))
   264→
   265→        try:
   266→            await self._verify_with_retry()
   267→        finally:
   268→            self.result.verifier.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   269→
   270→    @retry(
   271→        reraise=True,
   272→        stop=stop_after_attempt(2),
   273→        wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=1, max=10),
   274→        retry=retry_if_exception_type(VerifierTimeoutError),
   275→    )
   276→    async def _verify_with_retry(self) -> None:
   277→        try:
   278→            verifier = Verifier(
   279→                task=self._task,

   356→                await self._maybe_download_logs(
   357→                    source_dir=EnvironmentPaths.agent_dir.as_posix(),
   358→                    target_dir=self._trial_paths.agent_dir,

   366→                )

   390→
   391→            await self._maybe_download_logs(

    15→class BaseOrchestrator(ABC):
    16→    """
    17→    Handles deployment of multiple trials based on trial configs.
    18→
    19→    Needs to handle resuming, canceling, (pausing?), and finalizing.
    20→
    21→    TODO: Should this start an active process on the computer that runs until
    22→    completion? For now, yes. Otherwise how could it "finalize" the run?
    23→    """

    25→    def __init__(
    26→        self,
    27→        trial_configs: list[TrialConfig],

    29→        metrics: dict[str, list[BaseMetric]],
    30→        quiet: bool = False,

    39→            event: [] for event in TrialEvent
    40→        }

    29→class LocalOrchestrator(BaseOrchestrator):
    30→    """
    31→    Uses multiple processes to run trials in parallel on the local machine.
    32→    """
    33→
    34→    @staticmethod
    35→    def type() -> OrchestratorType:
    36→        """The type of orchestrator."""
    37→        return OrchestratorType.LOCAL
    38→
    39→    def __init__(
    40→        self,
    41→        trial_configs: list[TrialConfig],
    42→        n_concurrent_trials: int,
    43→        metrics: dict[str, list[BaseMetric]],
    44→        quiet: bool = False,
    45→        retry_config: RetryConfig | None = None,
    46→    ):
    47→        super().__init__(
    48→            trial_configs=trial_configs,
    49→            n_concurrent_trials=n_concurrent_trials,
    50→            metrics=metrics,
    51→            quiet=quiet,
    52→            retry_config=retry_config,
    53→        )
    54→        self._cache_tasks()
    55→        self._trial_results: list[TrialResult] = []
    56→        self._logger = logger.getChild(__name__)
    57→
    58→    def _cache_tasks(self):
    59→        git_configs = [
    60→            config for config in self._trial_configs if config.task.is_git_task()
    61→        ]
    62→
    63→        if not git_configs:
    64→            return
    65→
    66→        overwrites = {config.task.overwrite for config in git_configs}
    67→        output_dirs = {config.task.download_dir for config in git_configs}
    68→
    69→        if len(overwrites) > 1 or len(output_dirs) > 1:
    70→            raise ValueError(
    71→                "overwrite and output_dir cannot be different for different trials. "
    72→                "This should never happen."
    73→            )
    74→
    75→        client = TaskClient()
    76→
    77→        task_ids = [config.task.get_task_id() for config in git_configs]
    78→        client.download_tasks(
    79→            task_ids=task_ids,
    80→            overwrite=any(overwrites),
    81→            output_dir=output_dirs.pop() if output_dirs else None,
    82→        )
    83→
    84→    def _should_retry_exception(self, exception_type: str) -> bool:
    85→        if (
    86→            self._retry_config.exclude_exceptions
    87→            and exception_type in self._retry_config.exclude_exceptions
    88→        ):
    89→            self._logger.debug(
    90→                f"Exception {exception_type} is in exclude_exceptions, not retrying"
    91→            )
    92→            return False
    93→
    94→        if (
    95→            self._retry_config.include_exceptions
    96→            and exception_type not in self._retry_config.include_exceptions
    97→        ):
    98→            self._logger.debug(
    99→                f"Exception {exception_type} is not in include_exceptions, not retrying"
   100→            )
   101→            return False
   102→
   103→        return True
   104→
   105→    def _calculate_backoff_delay(self, attempt: int) -> float:
   106→        delay = self._retry_config.min_wait_sec * (
   107→            self._retry_config.wait_multiplier**attempt
   108→        )
   109→        return min(delay, self._retry_config.max_wait_sec)
   110→
   111→    def _setup_orchestrator_hooks(self, trial: Trial) -> None:
   112→        """Wire orchestrator-level hooks to the trial."""
   113→        for event, hooks in self._hooks.items():
   114→            for hook in hooks:
   115→                trial.add_hook(event, hook)
   116→
   117→    async def _execute_trial_with_retries(
   118→        self,
   119→        trial_config: TrialConfig,
   120→        setup_hooks=None,
   121→        on_retry=None,
   122→    ) -> TrialResult:
   123→        for attempt in range(self._retry_config.max_retries + 1):
   124→            if attempt > 0 and on_retry:
   125→                on_retry(attempt)
   126→
   127→            trial = Trial(trial_config)
   128→            self._setup_orchestrator_hooks(trial)
   129→
   130→            if setup_hooks:
   131→                setup_hooks(trial)
   132→
   133→            result = await trial.run()
   134→
   135→            if result.exception_info is None:
   136→                return result
   137→
   138→            if not self._should_retry_exception(result.exception_info.exception_type):
   139→                self._logger.debug(
   140→                    "Not retrying trial because the exception is not in "
   141→                    "include_exceptions or the maximum number of retries has been "
   142→                    "reached"
   143→                )
   144→                return result
   145→            if attempt == self._retry_config.max_retries:
   146→                self._logger.debug(
   147→                    "Not retrying trial because the maximum number of retries has been "
   148→                    "reached"
   149→                )
   150→                return result
   151→
   152→            shutil.rmtree(trial.trial_dir, ignore_errors=True)
   153→
   154→            delay = self._calculate_backoff_delay(attempt)
   155→
   156→            self._logger.debug(
   157→                f"Trial {trial_config.trial_name} failed with exception "
   158→                f"{result.exception_info.exception_type}. Retrying in "
   159→                f"{delay:.2f} seconds..."
   160→            )
   161→
   162→            await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   163→
   164→        raise RuntimeError(
   165→            f"Trial {trial_config.trial_name} produced no result. This should never "
   166→            "happen."
   167→        )
